rows.general_comments: - To request a reinspection, please contact the main office at 980-314-1620.

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P - Observed the PIC touch lo mein noodles with their barehands to check the temperature of the product while it was cooling down.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded all contaminated lo mein noodles.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed a large container of raw shrimp to be stored directly on top of containers of ginger on the speed rack in the WIC. REPEAT.
- Observed a container of raw shrimp to be stored on a shelf in the WIC directly above the large bus bin of lo mein noodles that were actively cooling. REPEAT.
- Observed a container of raw shrimp to be stored on the top shelf of the cookline lowboy refrigerator directly above opened packages of fries and dumplings containing precooked pork and chicken.
- Observed a container of raw beef to be stored on the top shelf of the cookline lowboy refrigerator directly above a container of lo mein noodles.

CDI - Storage order rearranged in the WIC, on the WIC speed rack, and in the cookline lowboy so that all RTE items are stored above all raw products.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P - Observed the dishwasher to be testing at 0ppm of chlorine at the time of the inspection. The chemical sanitizer bucket was found to be empty and in need of replacing.

CDI - PIC changed sanitizer bucket. After priming and a couple cycles the dishwasher was observed dispensing a proper 150ppm chlorine solution.

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. - Observed a large bus bin of cooked lo mein noodles to have missed the first cooling window while the EHS was on site. Cooked lo mein noodles were observed with a 6/9 date mark and a time stamp of 11:30am which was confirmed by the PIC and cookline employees to be when they had started to cool down the noodles. At 1:20pm the noodles were still observed at temperatures of 76F in the middle with a 5 degrees change over the course of the previous hour.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded bus bin of lo mein noodles.

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P - Observed a fryer basket full of sesame chicken to be at temperatures of 101-110F at the time of the inspection. Cookline employee stated that they fully cook the chicken and then hold it in the baskets until an order comes in at which point they would drop the chicken a second time.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded basket of chicken.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed insert containers of cooked baby corn and bean sprouts to be at temperatures of 60-65F in the cookline opentop refrigerator. PIC stated both items were placed up into the opentop at around 11am when the facility opened. Cookline employee stated that stated that the lid to the unit was opened for the entirety of their lunch rush, which had just ended as the EHS arrived. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC moved both items to the WIC to rapidly cool down.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed a large container of cooked shrimp in the WIC to not have a date mark of any kind. PIC stated that it was cooked yesterday afternoon.

CDI - PIC date marked item.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. - Observed no written procedures on site for the sushi rice that is held using TPHC. Item was observed properly time stamped and facility has had the document in the past but could not find it today.
- Observed no written procedures on site for the items at the wok line that are held on TPHC. As per the PIC the egg wash on the wok cart, the raw chicken, raw beef, raw shrimp, and cooked shrimp in the cookline opentop are all held using TPHC and are discarded if they last the 4 hours. All items were observed time stamped with an 11:30am time stamp except the egg wash, which was brought out of refrigeration at 11am when the facility opened. REPEAT.

CDI - Written procedures filled out for both the sushi rice and the wok line items at the end of the inspection. Egg wash time stamped for discard at 3pm

TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed a large bus bin of lo mein noodles to be cooling at a rate of .083 degrees/min and to miss the first cooling window with the noodles at 76F at the 2 hour mark. Bin was observed to be deeply filled, densely packed with noodles, and had plastic wrap covering the top of the container which all do not allow for adequate cold air flow around the product to facilitate proper cooling. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded due to it not reaching 70F within the first 2 hours of cooling.

- Observed approx 10-12 containers of smoked crab and a tray of kani to be observed at temperatures of 44-47F in the sushi counter lowboy refrigerator. As per the sushi employee all items were prepared that morning and immediately placed into the sushi lowboys to be used throughout the day. Date marks on the containers supported this statement. Containers of smoked crab were observed with tight fitting lids on and the tray of kani was observed with tight plastic wrap over the top of it, not allowing for adequate cold air flow around the product to facilitate proper cooling. Lowboy refrigerators are also not designed nor approved for rapid cooling and all cooling must be done in the WIC. REPEAT.
- Observed a container of cooked beef to be at a temperature of 94F in the cookline lowboy refrigerator and was observed to be actively cooling down per the cookline employee. Employee stated having cooked the item within the last hour before placing it in the lowboy, which is not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.
- Observed a container of lo mein noodles to be at a temperature of 66F in the cookline lowboy refrigerator and was observed to be actively cooling down per the cookline employee. Lo mein noodles were from the same bulk batch that was observed actively cooling in the WIC. Employees stated they needed noodles up at the cookline so they had taken from the batch still cooling and placed them in the cookline lowboy to use during lunch, which is not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.

CDI - PIC moved all items to the WIC to rapidly cool. Lids and plastic wrap removed from items.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. - Observed all secondary containers used on the wok line rolling cart for spices, seasonings, and sauces to not be labeled as to their contents.
- Observed the bulk bins of sugar, flour, MSG, and rice in the dry storage area to not be labeled as to their contents.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. - Observed the back door to the kitchen to not be fully closed, allowing for the potential entry of vermin into the facility. REPEAT.

CDI - Door closed.

- Observed a gap at the base of the back door when closed, allowing for the potential entry of vermin into the facility. REPEAT.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments:
4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. - Observed multiple stacks of plastic to go containers and lids at the cookline to be stored with their food contact surfaces face up, allowing for the potential contamination of the food contact surfaces.

4-502.13 Single-use and single-service articles cannot be reused. - Observed plastic single service containers to be in use as bulk bin scoops throughout the facility.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. - Observed a cutting board to have large chunks missing, heavy scarring, and heavy staining which rendered it no longer easily cleanable.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-501.18 Maintain wash, rinse, and sanitize solutions clean. - Observed the wash basin of the 3 compartment sink to be filled and active warewashing to be going on at the time of the inspection and the wash water was observed to be heavily contaminated with food debris/grime. All dishes go through the chemical dishwasher after being hand scrapped/washed.

CDI - PIC changed solution and EHS discussed with staff the proper times to change the wash water.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the racks used in the dishwasher to have heavy accumulations of black grime. REPEAT.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. - Observed a bin containing employee food and ingredients for employee meals such as raw pork to be stored on the middle shelf of the WIC, directly above boxes of raw vegetables to be used for consumers. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC relocated employee bin to lower shelf of the WIC.

rows.general_comments: EHS explain to PIC that they can call the MCHP to get a regrade if they so choose. Call MCHP at 980-314-1620

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-101.11 (A) PIC shall be present during all hours of operation. -PF Observed no PIC until mare then arrive half way threw inspection. PIC was unable to answer questions. 
2-102.11 PIC shall demonstrate knowledge of food borne disease prevention, application of the HACCP principles and the requirements of this code.-Pf. Observed PIC and establishment unable to demonstrate cold holding, cooling, cooling methods, food separation, insects control, maintaining facility clean. EHS will return in 10 days to verify training and cleanness of facility. 





Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed food employee filling up basin with water from hand sink. CDI - PIC to food employee to grab water from prep sink. 
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf Observed no hand soap at hand sink. Soap added.

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed. raw meat, cooked meat, and TCS vegetables stored without covering. CDI - coverings were placed on containers. 


3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw stake stored above RTE items in RIF. PSIC rearrange storage order to prevent contamination.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed raw chorizo, stored above fish. PIC rearrange storage order to prevent prevent contamination. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed heavy food debris and build up on utensils and equipment stored as clean. All items moved to 3 comp for proper washing. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed 4 salsa containers made from night before above 41 cooling in WIC missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed beef stock cooling from before in walk-in cooler above 41 missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P observed all items in prep unit cold holding above 41. PIC stated they will call maintenance to come out and look at unit. Establishment shall not use prep until unit is cold holding 41 and below. Establishment will work out out of reach in cooler until fixed. Ambient temp of unit is 48



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed several flies throughout preparation area and kitchen. PIC stated pest control comes out 1 times every 3 months. Increase pest control.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed queso stored on floor in walk-in cooler observed tortillas stored on floor in dry storage. CDI - PIC picked up off floor to prevent contamination.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed in use swiping cloth held in bleach sanitizer at 200ppm. CDI - PIC remade bucket at correct concentration.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed avocados in prep unit with stickers still on. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed heavy food, buildup, and debris on cooking equipment prep units, and RIC throughout the facility.
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed hand sink with huge water leak spilling on floor when hand sink is in use. Repair

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.110 Keep waste stored in receptacles so waste is inaccessible to insects and rodents. Observed trash, empty boxes, food, beer, mattress, out side back door of facility. Back of establishment needs to be cleaned and all items need to be put in trash can for avoid access to insects.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed a buildup of residue and debris on the floors and walls throughout the facility. Floors throughout kitchen and under units with heavy accumulation of debris, walls by the cook line and preperation aria by WIC in the back of the facility contained food debris and splatter. Increase cleaning frequency and thoroughness of floors walls and ceilings.

rows.violation_text:
1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-101.11 (A) PIC shall be present during all hours of operation. -PF Observed no PIC until mare then arrive half way threw inspection. PIC was unable to answer questions. 
2-102.11 PIC shall demonstrate knowledge of food borne disease prevention, application of the HACCP principles and the requirements of this code.-Pf. Observed PIC and establishment unable to demonstrate cold holding, cooling, cooling methods, food separation, insects control, maintaining facility clean. EHS will return in 10 days to verify training and cleanness of facility. 





Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed food employee filling up basin with water from hand sink. CDI - PIC to food employee to grab water from prep sink. 
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf Observed no hand soap at hand sink. Soap added.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed. raw meat, cooked meat, and TCS vegetables stored without covering. CDI - coverings were placed on containers. 


3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw stake stored above RTE items in RIF. PSIC rearrange storage order to prevent contamination.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. Observed raw chorizo, stored above fish. PIC rearrange storage order to prevent prevent contamination. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed heavy food debris and build up on utensils and equipment stored as clean. All items moved to 3 comp for proper washing. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

20 - Proper cooling time & temperatures: 3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed 4 salsa containers made from night before above 41 cooling in WIC missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed beef stock cooling from before in walk-in cooler above 41 missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.



For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P observed all items in prep unit cold holding above 41. PIC stated they will call maintenance to come out and look at unit. Establishment shall not use prep until unit is cold holding 41 and below. Establishment will work out out of reach in cooler until fixed. Ambient temp of unit is 48



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed salsa cooling in large deep plastic containers with tape, fitting lids and stacked. Observed beef stock, cool a large container. Both items missing cooling window. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed several flies throughout preparation area and kitchen. PIC stated pest control comes out 1 times every 3 months. Increase pest control.

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed queso stored on floor in walk-in cooler observed tortillas stored on floor in dry storage. CDI - PIC picked up off floor to prevent contamination.

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed in use swiping cloth held in bleach sanitizer at 200ppm. CDI - PIC remade bucket at correct concentration.

42 - Washing fruits & vegetables: 3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. Observed avocados in prep unit with stickers still on. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed heavy food, buildup, and debris on cooking equipment prep units, and RIC throughout the facility.

51 - Plumbing installed; proper backflow devices: 5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed hand sink with huge water leak spilling on floor when hand sink is in use. Repair

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.110 Keep waste stored in receptacles so waste is inaccessible to insects and rodents. Observed trash, empty boxes, food, beer, mattress, out side back door of facility. Back of establishment needs to be cleaned and all items need to be put in trash can for avoid access to insects.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed a buildup of residue and debris on the floors and walls throughout the facility. Floors throughout kitchen and under units with heavy accumulation of debris, walls by the cook line and preperation aria by WIC in the back of the facility contained food debris and splatter. Increase cleaning frequency and thoroughness of floors walls and ceilings.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no CFPM onsite during inspection. CDI- CFPM arrived during type up portion. 






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed handsink being used to stored container used to trap drain flies. CDI- PIC removed container to separate area and bean process of cleaning sink.

3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings.
Observed open bag of flour stored uncovered on lower shelf of prep table near walk-in. Cover or wrap bag.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed can opener blade heavily soiled during inspection. CDI- PIC removed item to be w/r/s. PIC stated new blades had been ordered. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P. Observed canned roasted red peppers being held out of temp for extended period for draining in prep sink. CDI- EHS discussed with PIC, PIC removed item to cool in low reach-in cooler on prep line. *REPEAT*



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
6-202.13 Properly locate mechanical insect control devices.
Fly glue trap stored directly over handsink and close to single-use pizza boxes. EHS discussed relocating. 
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry.
Observed front door maintained open without fly fan. Large windows adjacent had 1/2 fly fans on at start of inspection. *REPEAT*
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observd heavy presence of small flying insects in dish pit and can wash area. PIC stated pest control has been onsite weekly, in addition to establishment hiring floor and drain cleaners.

violations.comments:
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed hand sinks that were noticeably soiled with food debris/residue in the basins. Do not use hand sinks for any other purposes other than hand washing. (1 pt)

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed Raw shell eggs being stored above tubs of yogurt. -CDI- PIC relocated the raw shell eggs to the bottom shelf. (0 pts) 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
Observed TCS food items that were being held below 135F. -CDI- PIC decided to add those items to the TPHC list and will start using proper TPHC procedures. REPEAT*** (1.5 pts) 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed TCS food items throughout the facility holding above 41F while said to be cold holding. The units had ambient temperatures recorded at 38-40F. EHS noticed that the doors for the units were opened for prolonged periods of time likely raising the temperature of the items inside. -CDI- EHS coached PIC on keeping the doors closed as much as possible in order to maintain TCS food products at 41F and below. PIC closed the doors to allow the items to cool. (1.5 pts) 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed soiled cloths being stored throughout the facility on surfaces such as shelves and prep top units. Hold soiled cloths in a properly concentrated sanitizer solution. (0 pts)

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed base tiles coming off of the wall or completely missing throughout the facility. Repair necessary tiles on FRP walling. (0.5 pts)

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf-
Observed sticker residue on metal containers being stored as clean. CDI - PIC moved items to dish pit for w/r/s.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

-REPEAT-
3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. -Pf- 
Observed raw beef thawing under running water. Water temp was 78F. CDI - Item moved to walk-in cooler to thaw. EHS advised PIC that summer cold tap water temperatures are generally too warm to thaw items in the prep sink.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed scoop for semolina stored with handle in food. PIC moved scoop to dish pit for w/r/s and replaced with a clean scoop. All other scoops properly stored.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF 
Observed PIC not able to explain how new employees are trained in the employee health policy, and uncertain if there is a written policy onsite. 
CDI- County EHP and education provided. (-1)

REPEAT.
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pooled egg yolks above RTE foods and sliced produce in cook line lowboy. No contamination observed.
CDI- Eggs and RTE foods change positions so raw eggs are on bottom rack. (-1.5)

4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code, P. 
Observed meat slicer not yet used toward with food debris from last use behind blade. EHS advises staff to clean and sanitize equipment at required frequency.
CDI- Slicer w/r/s. (-1.5)

4-602.11 (A) (4) Food contact surface of a thermometer shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, before using or storing. P.
Observed probe thermometer in storage with food residue on probe. Increased cleaning frequency advised.
CDI- Probe sanitized.

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. 
Observed no written procedures for TPHC onsite.
CDI- EHS provided county form. 

FORM HERE: https://mecknc.widen.net/s/pvtc855zds/tphc-template-english

3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf
Observed fried chicken on TPHC without time label.
CDI- EHS verifies correct start temp >165F and allows PIC to mark for time. (-1.5)

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf
Observed raw chicken thawing on prep top at 45F.
CDI- EHS advises PIC on proper thawing methods. Chicken returned to WIC. (-0)

REPEAT.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf
Observed one fryer basket with metal wire coming apart from frame, no longer safe to use.
CDI- Basket voluntarily disposed and replaced with new. (-0)

REPEAT.
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized.
Observed funerals cutting boards with deep scoring, no longer cleanable. Replace at earliest opportunity. (-0.5) 

4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance.
Observed rusted metal racks inside WIC, no longer cleanable. Replace racks which are corroded; not smooth and cleanable.

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P- *Observed - Box of raw Chicken stored above plastic lidded container of marinated Raw Beef. *CDI - PIC corrected storage order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P- *Observed - Dish Machine chlorine sanitizer below proper concentration 10ppm. *CDI - EHS showed PIC how to prime dish machine to assist unit in achieving proper sanitizing concentration. Unit tested at 50-100ppm after priming.

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- *Observed - most foods in right flip top unit above 41F, see temp chart for details. PIC started all items were prepped this morning around 10-10:30AM. *CDI - PIC placed entire unit on TPHC and will only use make table of unit on TPHC moving forward until unit is repaired and can maintain proper cold holding temperatures. EHS will follow up with establishment to ensure food safety.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
*REPEAT* 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- *Observed - TCS items in Right Flip Top Unit above 41F, see temp chart for details. PIC stated items were cooling in flip top unit from prep this morning around 10:30AM. PIC stated unit was repaired a week or 2 ago and unit was fine this morning upon their arrival this morning when they checked it. Establishment utilizing unit thats in disrepair for cooling. *CDI - PIC moved all items in units lowboy to other refrigeration units to improve cooling rate. Items in Make Table were placed onto 4 hour TPHC Time hold. Items will be discarded at 2:30PM. PIC already possesses written procedures from a previous inspection.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. *Observed - Soy Sauce, Fish Oil and 2 other squeeze bottles without labels to properly identify substances within. *CDI - PIC labeled items.

Do not interrupt the cooking process without approved procedures. -P- Observed raw hamburgers cooked on the grill with a final cook temp of 149F-165F depending on the patty and part of the patty cooked. PIC had food employee drop another batch of burgers, burgers were cooked to 149F-165F again. -CDI- All burgers were voluntarily discarded. PIC adjusted the automatic clamshell grill to cook for longer. 
Burgers were properly cooked after adjustment.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed lidded prepped foods such as lettuce and tomato’s that were from prep approximately 1 hour prior in the lowboy cooler -CDI- Once PIC observed the foods from prep cooling in the lowboy he directed a food employee to go and cool the foods in the WIC. Foods were cooling properly by the end of the inspection.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Several TCS RTE foods in walk-in cooler were missing prep date labels. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days of preparation. CDI - Employee discarded items that she didn't know prep date and most of the other foods were prepared yesterday so she put yesterday's date on items. REPEAT

rows.general_comments:
Observed raw meat stored over onions on Don coyotl. Observed raw shelled eggs commingled with shredded cheese in WIC #2. Observed raw shrimp over potatoes at ace of spuds in WIC #2. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. PIC was in process of daily walk through and had already placed “parking tickets” at some of the shared spaces, which indicates to the individual PICs that these items need to be corrected.

Observed one PIC in WIC #2 cooling rice with protective plastic coverings and stacked on top of one another. EHS educated PIC about proper cooling methods and items unstacked and protective coverings removed.

violations.comments:
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. Observed 4 handwashing sinks without handwashing signs. -CDI- PIC posted handsink signage. -0-
6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf- Observed one handwashing sink with no soap. -CDI- PIC replenished soap supply. 
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf- Observed one handsink with no paper towels. -CDI- PIC replenished paper towel supply.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris on shared slicer stored as clean. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. -1.5-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. Observed dark buildup on inside of ice machine at ice chute. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). In WIC observed no date mark on housemade broth in at Mazali, rice and vegetable mix with no dates at sabor limon, sliced watermelon date marked 6/13 (discard 6/20), and bacon wrapped watermelon 6/15 (discard 6/21) at Solo, macaroni noodles 6/6 (discard 6/12), rice 6/10 (discard 6/16) at crown and tide, and seafood rice 6/7 (discard 6/13), noodles with no date mark at Mem’s Thai RIC, and heavy cream date marked 5/13 (discard 5/20) at Grey’s RIC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed flying pests throughout. Per PIC, pest control frequency has increased. **REPEAT** -1-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-402.11 Fixed equipment shall be spaced to allow access for cleaning around the equipment or sealed to adjoining equipment or walls. Observed some sinks in need of recaulking/resealing. PIC stated that she had removed some of the caulking because it is set to be repaired this week. -0-
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed gaskets lifting from door of WIC. Repair/replace. Per PIC, gaskets have been ordered. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed some rusted shelving.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tops of dish machine and some equipment beneath hoods in need of cleaning. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. -0-

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed dust buildup on some ceilings and vent covers throughout. Observed top of paper towel dispenser by handsink at BOH/near dishpit/3-comp sink in need of cleaning. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed detached baseboard throughout shared kitchen in need of repair. Per PIC, work order has been placed for baseboards and set to be repaired this week. **REPEAT** -0.5-

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf- Observed one handsink with no paper towels. -CDI- PIC replenished paper towel supply.

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris on shared slicer stored as clean. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. -1.5-

4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. Observed dark buildup on inside of ice machine at ice chute. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended.

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). In WIC observed no date mark on housemade broth in at Mazali, rice and vegetable mix with no dates at sabor limon, sliced watermelon date marked 6/13 (discard 6/20), and bacon wrapped watermelon 6/15 (discard 6/21) at Solo, macaroni noodles 6/6 (discard 6/12), rice 6/10 (discard 6/16) at crown and tide, and seafood rice 6/7 (discard 6/13), noodles with no date mark at Mem’s Thai RIC, and heavy cream date marked 5/13 (discard 5/20) at Grey’s RIC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed some rusted shelving.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed detached baseboard throughout shared kitchen in need of repair. Per PIC, work order has been placed for baseboards and set to be repaired this week. **REPEAT** -0.5-

violations.comments: 3-304.11 (A) Food shall only contact surfaces of properly cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils. -P Observed two (2) portions of food (grilled chicken and diced tomatoes) in relatively clean containers in prep unit 3 nesting within spinach bin. CDI - PIC removed containers from food and rotated to prevent potential contamination.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed three (3) out of date, non date mark exempt TCS food items (burrata prepared 6/13, sliced tomatoes prepared 6/14, and undated meatballs) in prep line 1 (closest to bar.) Per conversation with PIC, items without dates in prep units were prepared on day inspection and are to be held for less than 24 hours. CDI - PIC voluntarily disposed of food held for greater than 7 days.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Observed one (1) employee preparing food (lasagna trays, etc.) while wearing wrist band. REPEAT.

Note: No escalation in point deduction due to some improvement; only one (1) food employee was observed with hand, arm, or wrist jewelry.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed one (1) in use wiping cloth held at prep line, above in use wiping cloth bucket. CDI - Bucket was remade and calibrated with at 3 compartment sink and wiping cloths was returned to bucket.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. - Observed one (1) container of water used to hold in use utensils at the cookline in an insert being held 124F water on the prep counter at the time of the inspection. All other water containers used to hold in use utensils were observed at 135F or above. PIC has been informed that they must keep the insert container of water at 135F or to not use water to store in use utensils.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed approximately six (6) clean containers stored on drying rack in a manner that did not facilitate air drying (wet stacking). CDI - Containers were separated out to allow for air drying. REPEAT.

Note: EHS initially observed high temperature dish machine failing to hit 160F on high temperature dish plates. After rerunning machine, final temperatures above 160F were observed. PIC has stated that repair personnel have been contacted.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments:
2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur.
EHS observed two employee beverages stored on shelves above prep area and expo area. 
CDI- PIC relocated the drinks to an approved location. 
-0-

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P 
EHS observed a pan of cheese whiz in hot holding cabinet at 125F. 
CDI- PIC reheated the cheese to 165F and placed it back into the hot box. 
-0- 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
EHS observed several items in prep unit #3 that were holding above 41F. PIC stated that a temperature check was conducted at 11AM and all food was at 41F. PIC instructed food employee to place items on ice to help rapidly cool them. Ambient air temperature was 49F in the lower portion of the unit and 47F in the upper portion. After approximately 45 minutes items in the cooler had began to cool, however the ambient air temperature of the unit is still at 44F. 
CDI- PIC is placing entire unit on ice - Emergency TPHC if needed. 3- day VR Required. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed a burrito in the hot holding drawer at 127F. PIC stated that the burrito would be discarded after a 30 minute time hold. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the burrito. Ensure that all foods in hot holding are maintained at 135F and above. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed ranch cups that were prepped approximately an hour prior to the EHS arrival. Ranch cups were present in the front of house lowboy cooler with lids on and stacked together. The ranch’s were recorded at 44F, 30 minutes later a second temperature was recorded at 44F. -CDI- PIC moved the ranch cups to cool in the WIC, ranch cups were properly cooling by the end of the inspection. Ensure proper cooling methods such as venting cooling foods and using adequate cooling equipment. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed several food employees with no hair restraints present upon EHSs arrival to the facility. -CDI- Food employees placed a hair restraint on after inspection began. 
Ensure all food employees who are working with food or food contact surfaces are wearing some form of hair restraint.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed a build up of food debris and residue on the batter station NFCSs, on the sides of cooking equipment, and on the sides of holding equipment. Ensure that all non-food contact surfaces are cleaned and maintained as clean.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed a floor tile missing upon entry into WIC 1, observed small chips in floor tiles throughout the facility. Observed low grout present on the floors throughout the facility. 
Ensure that floors are designed to be smooth and easily cleanable.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P -Observed raw bacon stored over ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the prep unit cooler. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the raw bacon.
-REPEAT-


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. -Observed food residue among multiple dishes, stored as clean. -CDI- Dishes were moved back to dish pit to be re-washed/rinsed/sanitized. 
-REPEAT- No point escalation due improvement in sticker residue since last inspection. EHS observed no sticker residue present on clean dishes. PIC stated facility switched to dissolvable stickers.-


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P -Observed queso holding below 135F in hot holding steam well. -CDI- Because queso was originally cooked less than one hour ago, PIC reheated queso to 165F within 30 minutes.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 

-Observed hard boiled eggs above 41F in prep top cooler. Observed eggs above fill-line of container. Top portion of hard boiled eggs were above 41F, while bottom portion of eggs (below fill-line) was properly cold holding below 41F. PIC stated eggs were filled this morning, less than one hour ago. -CDI- PIC removed top half of hard boiled eggs and placed in WIC for rapid cooling. Going forward, facility will not fill hard boiled eggs above fill line to ensure proper cold holding.

-Observed portioned cups of buttermilk ranch and chipotle ranch left at room temperature on server expo line. PIC stated they hold the portioned cups at room temperature all day. Both ranches contain sour cream and buttermilk. -CDI- PIC placed the chipotle ranch and buttermilk ranch on 4-hour TPHC, and added both food items to 4-hour TPHC procedures. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed multiple foods throughout facility without a date mark (grilled mushrooms, corn, sliced tomatoes, diced tomatoes, queso, jalapeno cheddar sauce, cooked noodles, hot-brined pickled red onions, hot dogs, mozzarella cheese, pimento cheese, black bean burgers, beyond burgers, deviled egg mix, spring mix, romaine). 
-CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the cooked noodles and pickled onions, as PIC could not verify prep dates for those.
-CDI- PIC verified prep/cook dates for all other food items by discussing with chef and referencing large batches in WIC and properly date marked all foods listed above, except noodles and pickled onions.
REPEAT


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. -Observed flour/breader containers without labels. Label all containers with the common name of the food inside.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. -Observed multiple flies throughout kitchen during inspection. EHS recommends increasing frequency of pest control services during summer months to reduce insect presence.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. -Observed container of deviled eggs directly on floor in WIC.

6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf -Observed two heavily dented cans stored among cans intended for customer use. -CDI- PIC removed cans from shelves and placed in office for discard or credit.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. -Observed in-use wiping cloth stored directly on prep top surface, not within sanitizer solution. -CDI- PIC placed wiping cloth is soiled laundry bin.
3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use. -Observed sticker intact on avocado that has been cut and placed in prep cooler. Remove stickers during the wash process.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed. -Observed bowls used for scooping breader mixture submerged within the food. -CDI- PIC removed bowls from breader.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. -Observed numerous stacks of clean dishing being stored on top of each other wet. EHS advised to stagger dishes to allow for adequate air drying prior to storage.
REPEAT

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. -Observed interior surfaces of reach-in coolers, freezers, and cooler gaskets with visible accumulation of food residue. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation.
REPEAT

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. -Observed floors in WIC with buildup of food debris. Increase frequency of cleaning.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. -Observed employee food stored above customer food in prep unit cooler. Observed employee bag stored above onion bags in dry storage. -CDI- PIC relocated employee belongings to prevent contamination. 
REPEAT

rows.violation_text:
15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P -Observed raw bacon stored over ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the prep unit cooler. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the raw bacon.
-REPEAT-


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. -Observed food residue among multiple dishes, stored as clean. -CDI- Dishes were moved back to dish pit to be re-washed/rinsed/sanitized. 
-REPEAT- No point escalation due improvement in sticker residue since last inspection. EHS observed no sticker residue present on clean dishes. PIC stated facility switched to dissolvable stickers.-


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

21 - Proper hot holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P -Observed queso holding below 135F in hot holding steam well. -CDI- Because queso was originally cooked less than one hour ago, PIC reheated queso to 165F within 30 minutes.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 

-Observed hard boiled eggs above 41F in prep top cooler. Observed eggs above fill-line of container. Top portion of hard boiled eggs were above 41F, while bottom portion of eggs (below fill-line) was properly cold holding below 41F. PIC stated eggs were filled this morning, less than one hour ago. -CDI- PIC removed top half of hard boiled eggs and placed in WIC for rapid cooling. Going forward, facility will not fill hard boiled eggs above fill line to ensure proper cold holding.

-Observed portioned cups of buttermilk ranch and chipotle ranch left at room temperature on server expo line. PIC stated they hold the portioned cups at room temperature all day. Both ranches contain sour cream and buttermilk. -CDI- PIC placed the chipotle ranch and buttermilk ranch on 4-hour TPHC, and added both food items to 4-hour TPHC procedures. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

-Observed multiple foods throughout facility without a date mark (grilled mushrooms, corn, sliced tomatoes, diced tomatoes, queso, jalapeno cheddar sauce, cooked noodles, hot-brined pickled red onions, hot dogs, mozzarella cheese, pimento cheese, black bean burgers, beyond burgers, deviled egg mix, spring mix, romaine). 
-CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the cooked noodles and pickled onions, as PIC could not verify prep dates for those.
-CDI- PIC verified prep/cook dates for all other food items by discussing with chef and referencing large batches in WIC and properly date marked all foods listed above, except noodles and pickled onions.
REPEAT

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed sliced roast beef in hot tray between 126F-137F other hot tray was holding at 137F throughout. For the tray below 135F PIC stated that the tray had been in HH for 1 Hour. -CDI- PIC moved the tray into the oven and reheated the roast beef to above 165F before placing back onto HH. Ensure that all foods on HH are maintained at 135F or above. REPEAT



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed tomatoes, caramelized onions, and gyro above 41F in the make station unit. PIC stated that the foods went into the unit this morning at approximately 10:00 PM. -CDI- PIC moved foods above 41F to WIC to cool down, foods cooled by end of the inspection. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed facility cooling sandwiches and sandwich ingredients in reach in coolers tightly wrapped with no significant change in temperature after 35 minutes (see temperature log). The reach in where food was being cooled was also reading as an ambient temperature around 60F. CDI - Food was moved to cool in WIC uncovered or voluntarily discarded. 


For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed egg and tomato at small kitchen reach in reading over 41F. Observed foods in large kitchen reach in reading over 41F. CDI - Foods from large kitchen reach in were voluntarily discarded. Foods from the small kitchen reach in will be placed on TPHC as they were made for the lunch and not properly cooled before placing them in cold holding refrigerator. All leftover food shall be voluntarily discarded after 12:30 lunch. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed food cooling in reach covered or tightly wrapped or packaged. CDI - Food was moved to cool in WIC or uncovered. EHS also recommended using the WIF if in need of faster cooling.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF. Observed PIC unable to name required symptoms and illnesses, unable to proved signed copy of employee health procedure provided by health department. CDI- EHS discussed with PIC and present employees, provided employee health handout. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). SEE COMMENT BELOW.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed imitation crab in reach-in freezer repackaged, salsa repackaged from can to container, and sliced tomatoes prepped on yesterday all stored without a date label. CDI- EHS discussed with PIC importance of date labeling items when removed from original packaging and frozen to be used on later date, PIC added date labels to listed items. *REPEAT*



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). SEE COMMENT BELOW.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed imitation crab in reach-in freezer repackaged, salsa repackaged from can to container, and sliced tomatoes prepped on yesterday all stored without a date label. CDI- EHS discussed with PIC importance of date labeling items when removed from original packaging and frozen to be used on later date, PIC added date labels to listed items. *REPEAT*



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P
EHS observed food employee crack rawshell eggs and without washing hands or donning. New gloves grabbed plate to plate ready to eat foods. 
CDI- EHS intervened before eggs were plated and employee discarded gloves, properly washed hands and got new plates. 
-2-

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. 
Observed wiping cloth stored in sanitizer solution at 100ppm. EHS and PIC determined that the chemical dispenser at the 3-compartment sink was not functioning properly. PIC will instruct employees on how to hand mix sanitizer until work order is fulfilled. 
**REPEAT** -0.5-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying.
EHS observed a large amount of metal container and other dishes stacked wet. 
-0-

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. 
Observed low grout throughout facility. 
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. 
Observed food buildup on ceiling tiles in prep area. Observed dust buildup on AC/vent covers throughout kitchen. Observed build up on walls in dish pit.
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed broken/cracked and loose floor tiles throughout. 
**REPEAT** -1-
