violations.comments: 5-202.12 Provide at least 100F water at handsinks. Observed PIC state hot water issue in morning. Observed hot water between 78-79 degrees F in restroom and kitchen handwashing sinks. Repeat.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed 1 container of guacamole with cut tomato overstocked at 51 degrees F above chill line on container. CDI, overstocked guacamole relocated to rapidly cool.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments:
3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P
Observed various odiferous raw meats in various coolers during inspection.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.
1 pt.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed various plates and bowls being stored as clean on line during inspection.
CDI - All items placed into dish pit. Additional protection from contaminants necessary to preclude further violations. Risk Control Plan may be discussed if no improvement by next inspection.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B)(4)(5)Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. The food shall be discarded after the food has completed its 4 hour hold time, if the food is in unmarked containers, or marked with a time that exceeds the 4 hour time limit. -P
Observed charred lemons that had been held on TPHC on 6/17/26 cooled down to be placed back on TPHC on 6/18/26.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.
0 pts.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed marinated calamari cooling from ambient while in a deep, sealed plastic container. Calamari was not cooling hastily.
CDI - PIC moved calamari to uncovered, shallow metal container. Cooling rate hastened.
Repeat. 1 pt.
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments:
3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf
Observed no shell stock tags on site for mussels served on specials menu. PIC stated they were unaware how long tags needed to be retained, and discarded tags the day prior to inspection.
CDI - Facility no longer offers special with mussels. REHS educated PIC on proper shellstock tag retention.
0 pts.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed opened container of chicken stock base being held outside of refrigeration despite packaging stating it must be kept refrigerated after opening. Observed all TCS foods in grab n go cooler and display case holding above 41F. See temperature chart. Ambient in grab n go 52F. Ambient in display case 44F. Staff maintained temperature logs verify that unit temperatures began rising earlier in the day. 
CDI - Maintenance on site during inspection. All out of temperature items voluntarily discarded. Facility ceased the use of grab n go until repaired. Display case only stocked with non-TCS foods until repair.
Repeat. 3 pts.
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed several TCS items in prep unit holding over 41F. PIC stated they took temperatures around 9 am. violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed no CFPM present at the facility during time of the inspection. PIC who is a CFPM was not working the shift today.
Ensure there is a CFPM present during all hours of operation.

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed 2 vegetable peelers soiled with debris on blades, stored on the clean utensil hooks.
CDI: Vegetable peelers were taken to the dish machine to be washed/rinsed/sanitized.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed one pan of roasted cauliflower date-marked with a prep date of 6/10, stored inside the lowboy portion of make table 1. Observed one pan of roasted cauliflower date-marked with a prep date of 6/11, stored inside the lowboy portion of make table 1. Food employee stated these were the correct date of prep and are not frozen.
CDI: Food employee voluntarily discarded both pans of roasted cauliflower, since they had surpassed the 7-day window.
Observed a pan of marinated cut tomatoes date-marked with a prep date of 6/10, stored inside lowboy cooler 2. Food employee stated they batch prep this food item and freeze it before thawing out prior to serving. Food employee stated he pulled this out of the freezer 2 days prior.
CDI: Food employee created second date label with thaw date of 6/16 to depict the "clock start date."
Observed a cambro full of roasted mix veggies that was date-marked with a prep date of 6/09, stored inside the lowboy portion of make table 2. Food employee stated that was the correct date of prep and is not frozen.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded cambro, since it had surpassed the 7-day window.
Observed a pan of roasted smashed potatoes date-marked with a prep date of 6/10, stored inside the WIC. PIC stated this was the correct date of prep.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded potatoes, since it had surpassed the 7-day window.
Observed a pan of braised beef date-marked with a prep date of 6/10, stored inside the WIC. PIC stated this was the correct date of prep, but was frozen then thawed out 2 days prior.
CDI: PIC created second date label with thaw date of 6/16 to depict the "clock start date."
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed one menu item on facility's brunch menu "Roasted vegetable grain bowl" that includes two eggs over easy, missing an asterisk for their consumer advisory.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance for their consumer advisory.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed two unlabeled spray bottles full of liquids stored on the chemical dish rack; one bottle had a label where it had been labeled at one point but had smeared off, and the other was completely unlabeled. PIC stated both spray bottles were full of sanitizer.
CDI: Food employee labeled both spray bottles for easy identification to prevent contamination.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P EHS observed half pot of cooked white rice being held under 135F in rice cooker. PIC found unit half plugged in and stated it possibly turned off after the cooking process by mistake. PIC stated rice was cooked at 10am. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded rice. (1.5)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) EHS observed cut salmon and spicy crab mix in 2-door LB sushi unit without proper date marks. PIC stated it was prepared the day prior and confirmed with sushi chef. CDI- PIC allowed to date the salmon and spicy crab mix, Chef verified with sushi chef items were prepared prior to close the night before. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.115 Discard items and litter stored in the waste areas and enclosures as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance. EHS observed litter accumulating around dumpster area. EHS observed items such as shelving and wood pallets being stored outside of dumpster area. EHS advised to clear out area of unnecessary items and litter. (0)

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed large amount of residue on the ceiling in hibachi dining rooms due to preparing food during hibachi service. EHS advised to increase cleaning frequency of ceilings and walls of dining rooms due to food being prepared in room. (0)

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P. Observed two packages of cucumbers and one pepper with mold on them in the WIC. CDI- PIC had those food items removed for discard.

3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed containers of unwashed produce being stored over ready to eat food items (cooked chicken, ceviche, etc.) in the WIC. Ready to eat food items need to not be stored under unwashed produce to prevent possible contamination. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf. Observed squeeze bottle of TCS spicy mayo and lime cream made in-house not properly marked with 4 hour time limit. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the spicy mayo/ crema and brought out a new bottle with proper time stamp.

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments: 3-603.11 (B)(1) Consumer Advisory Disclosures shall indicate that the animal-derived food is or can be served raw or undercooked by using words and phrases such as raw, undercooked, cooked to order, etc., or by asterisking to a footnote per 3-603.11 (B)(2). -Pf. Observed the ceviche listed on the menu is made with raw farm raised shrimp and needs to have an asterisk next to it as a disclosure.VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-402.11 Fixed equipment shall be spaced to allow access for cleaning around the equipment or sealed to adjoining equipment or walls. Observed the left side drain board at the dishwasher is not sealed to the wall. Seal drain board with sealant to prevent splash, etc. from getting behind it.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed filters on side of ice machine have dust build-up and need to be cleaned. Repeat. Points not escalated due to equipment item needing cleaned not being same one from previous inspection.

violations.comments:
3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. 
Observed Taziki sauce cooling at 47 degrees F at 2:36 pm in deep covered container. At 3:00 taziki sauce was at 46 degrees F. CDI, sauce transferred to shallow uncovered container to rapidly cool.

For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .
3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed chicken cooling in deep covered container at 57 degrees F at 2:22. At 3:00 chicken remained at 57 degrees F. CDI, chicken relocated to shallow uncovered containers to rapidly cool. Repeat.


For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed cut lettuce cold held at 51 degrees F. CDI, lettuce voluntarily discarded. 


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 

Observed Taziki sauce cooling at 47 degrees F at 2:36 pm in deep covered container. At 3:00 taziki sauce was at 46 degrees F. CDI, sauce transferred to shallow uncovered container to rapidly cool.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed wet wiping cloth on cutting board.

violations.comments:
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed gnocchi (6/9) held past its 7-day timeframe. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the gnocchi.
-Observed vegetable stock with two date marks on the container (6/3 and 6/16). -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the stock.
-Observed pomodoro sauce with an illegible date mark, that appeared to state 6/10. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the sauce.
-Observed hard boiled eggs without a date mark. -CDI- PIC verified eggs were cooked yesterday and properly added a date mark.
-Observed multiple foods in WIC with improper date marks (ex.- bread dough with label of 11/18). -CDI- PIC confirmed all improperly labeled date marks were prepared within the past 7 days and will correct date marks/train employees on proper date marking procedures.


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. 
Observed PIC ServSafe expired in February. No CFPM onsite. (-1)

TEN DAY VR.
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed dark residue inside ice machine. Wash and sanitize at earliest opportunity. EHS will verify within 10 days. (-1.5)

4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure
Observed food splatter inside cook line microwave. Increase cleaning frequency.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed overfilled container of baked potatoes and commercially prepackaged steaks in cook line fliptops above 41F. 
CDI- EHS verified items were moved from WIC to fliptop this morning. Baked potatoes voluntarily disposed, raw steak brought to RIF for rapid cooling. EHS advises staff not to put food into containers above the fill line. (-0)

TEN DAY VR.
4-302.12 Provide an accessible thermometer for use. Provide a thin probe thermometer for accurate measure of thin foods.-Pf .
Observed probe thermometer without batteries. EHS advises replaces batteries and providing a back up thermometer. Verification will be made within 10 days. (-0.5)

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests.
Observed German Cockroach on wall to left side of cooking equipment. All area’s of kitchen, food prep, and storage area’s were carefully examined for other signs of pests. No signs, or contamination to food/equipment, observed. PIC confirmed establishment receives routine pest control treatments, and was advised to increase their frequency. (-0)

3-302.15 Wash fruits and vegetables prior to use.
Observed avocados stated to be washed in lowboy with stickers still remaining. 
CDI- PIC returns avocados to veg wash sink for re-washing. (-0)

Observed fryer basket with deteriorated wire, no longer safe to use. EHS advises PIC to remove basket from service and procure replacement in good repair. (-1)


4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent.
Observed portioned TCS sauces stored inside Coca Cola mini cooler graded for storage of commercially prepackaged items only. TCS foods inside observed below 41F. Replace with approved equipment when possible.

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P

Observed employee handle raw salmon, change gloves with no handwash, and then handle single service items. CDI: Employee was educated on when to properly Washburn hands. Employee washed hands and donned new gloves.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. Pf Observed 2 dented cans of salsa in basement storage area on the can rack. CDI: Cans removed to designated area for return.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed cold smoked salmon stored over ready to eat dressings in salad walk in. CDI: Items reorganized. 
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over ready to eat items at juice station. Observed unwashed berries stored over pickled peppers in reach in part of cooler at nourish station. CDI: Items reorganized. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

violations.description: Proper reheating procedures for hot holding
violations.comments: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed pan of meatballs reheated to 130F. CDI: Meatballs placed back in steamer and reheated to over 165F.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed sea bass, short rib, and mashed potatoes in hotbox for a catering held below 135F. Hot box was at 134F per digital face on unit. CDI: Items rapidly reheated to over 165F. Hot box moved to 170F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed sliced and shredded cheese in cooler drawers, and pasteurized eggs and pasteurized egg whites in reach in cooler above 41F. CDI: Items moved to walk in cooler to cool. Ambient air temps and temps of other items in different areas of the unit were at or below 41F. Ensure units are not overloaded with items preventing air flow and that cooler drawers are closed completely between use. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P Observed calzones pulled to TPHC prior to reaching 41F. CDI: Product returned to walk in to continue cooling. 
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf Observed no time marked for lettuce or quinoa at nourish station.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed wiping cloth in QUAT sanitizer solution at 100ppm. -CDI- PIC refilled with properly concentrated sanitizer. Observed cloths stored in buckets of soapy water. CDI: Cloths removed. REPEAT VIOLATION

violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed a few wet stacked pans stored as clean in the prep kitchen area. CDI: Pans taken back to the dish area for cleaning.

Increase cleaning frequency. Observed 2 pans with sticker residue on exterior of the pans. CDI: Pans taken to dish area. REPEAT VIOLATION Some improvement from previous inspection due to doors and handles and other exterior surfaces of equipment being cleaned and maintained better.

13 - Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated: 3-202.15 Food packaging must be in good condition, intact and protect the food inside. Pf Observed 2 dented cans of salsa in basement storage area on the can rack. CDI: Cans removed to designated area for return.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed cold smoked salmon stored over ready to eat dressings in salad walk in. CDI: Items reorganized. 
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over ready to eat items at juice station. Observed unwashed berries stored over pickled peppers in reach in part of cooler at nourish station. CDI: Items reorganized. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

19 - Proper reheating procedures for hot holding: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed pan of meatballs reheated to 130F. CDI: Meatballs placed back in steamer and reheated to over 165F.

21 - Proper hot holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed sea bass, short rib, and mashed potatoes in hotbox for a catering held below 135F. Hot box was at 134F per digital face on unit. CDI: Items rapidly reheated to over 165F. Hot box moved to 170F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed sliced and shredded cheese in cooler drawers, and pasteurized eggs and pasteurized egg whites in reach in cooler above 41F. CDI: Items moved to walk in cooler to cool. Ambient air temps and temps of other items in different areas of the unit were at or below 41F. Ensure units are not overloaded with items preventing air flow and that cooler drawers are closed completely between use. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

24 - Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P Observed calzones pulled to TPHC prior to reaching 41F. CDI: Product returned to walk in to continue cooling. 
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf Observed no time marked for lettuce or quinoa at nourish station.

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed wiping cloth in QUAT sanitizer solution at 100ppm. -CDI- PIC refilled with properly concentrated sanitizer. Observed cloths stored in buckets of soapy water. CDI: Cloths removed. REPEAT VIOLATION

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed a few wet stacked pans stored as clean in the prep kitchen area. CDI: Pans taken back to the dish area for cleaning.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed smaller ice machine in produce area not functioning. Repair or replace ice machine.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed accumulation of food debris/residue on non-food contact surfaces such as gaskets of reach in coolers, inside of coolers such as prep units and walk ins throughout facility. Increase cleaning frequency. Observed 2 pans with sticker residue on exterior of the pans. CDI: Pans taken to dish area. REPEAT VIOLATION Some improvement from previous inspection due to doors and handles and other exterior surfaces of equipment being cleaned and maintained better.

rows.general_comments:
Cold holding on the makeline has been out constantly during inspections, likely due to the broken lids on the equipment as it is not able to maintain a tight seal. If cold holding is found to be out of compliance during next routine inspection facility will be placed on a risk control plan for cold holding. 

EHS advised that permit states “This FOOD ESTABLISHMENT, when providing an open air atmosphere by use of garage doors, open wall or other means to a dining area must in operation effective measures in to prevent the presence of insects, rodents or other public health pests. The roll-up doors in the dining area are to be secured at all times until an air curtain or screens are installed.” EHS told employees they are not permitted to open garage door until air curtain is installed.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
EHS observed Alfredo, raw beef, cooked chicken above 41F. 
CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded Alfredo and chicken. EHS allowed PIC to cool raw beef in freezer. 
**REPEAT** -3- 
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf
EHS observed facility holding cooked chicken wings on time with no sticker to denote time when product was taken out of cold holding. 
CDI- EHS allowed PIC to date the product as they pulled items out for an order which showed on the ticket. 
-0-

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed several items in make unit that were above 41F at the start of inspection. PIC stated the the unit had just been cleaned and that this items were left outside the unit for approximately 10-15 minutes while the unit wa being cleaned. PIC stated the items had been placed back into the unit to cool. After 30 minutes there was no a change in temperature of these products. Ambient air temperature of the unit was 41F. 
CDI- All items were relocated to the walk in cooler where they began to cool. See temp chart. 
-0.5- SEE COMMENTS

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry.
EHS observed garage door open without the use of an air curtain.
-0-

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. 
Observed boxes of food stored on the floor in the walk in freezer. **REPEAT** -1-
6-404.11 Designate a separate area for items that are not for use and are being held for credit, such as dented cans or recalled products. -Pf
Observed severely dented can stored in dry storage area with other non-dented products. 
CDI- PIC relocated can to hold for credit.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. 
Observed pizza prep unit flip top in disrepair during the inspection. Lids are not able to be closed and form a tight seal. Repair/replace.
Observed a leak in the walk in cooler. EHS observed that walk in freezer trim is in disrepair which is leading to additional ice build-up..
**REPEAT** -1-

violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. 
EHS observed employee belongings handing from racks were facility food is stored, garments are contacting facility food

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P EHS observed raw calamari stored over RTE foods in make unit reach in compartment. CDI - PIC rearranged storage order, no contamination observed. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. EHS observed raw calamari and raw turkey being prepped/thawed at three compartment sink with no visible separation or protection. PIC stated raw ground turkey was left at three compartment sink drain board to drain excess liquid. EHS observed raw calamari in the next compartment over thawing at room temperature. EHS advised to utilize prep sinks when preparing raw proteins and avoid using three compartment sink for these tasks. Facility has two prep sinks; one is a two compartment sink. CDI - PIC removed calamari and placed at empty prep sink under running water; ground turkey was moved to WIC. No contamination observed. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation(1.5)

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed numerous pans and plastic containers stacked on top of each other, stored as clean, with visible sticker residue present on pans. CDI - PIC removed all pans with residue to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. EHS observed buffalo chicken dip in WIC at 44F. PIC stated dip was prepared yesterday and placed into WIC. PIC stated item has not left WIC since being placed there yesterday. EHS observed dip with tight fitting lid. EHS observed ground beef patties being cooled from preparation 3.5 hours prior to inspection. EHS observed patties at 45F with 15 minutes left to go, tightly covered with plastic wrap and lid. EHS intervened and advised PIC to move patties to freezer to quickly cool, so that the cooling parameter could be met. PIC stated temperatures have not been taken of these items during cooling process. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded chicken dip, patties spread out and placed into WIF uncovered. EHS observed patties at 41, 15 minutes later. 
For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .


3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. EHS observed queso in WIC above 41F. PIC stated queso was made yesterday. Queso is split into smaller metal containers and left in WIC uncovered to cool. EHS observed film over queso, PIC stated item was not stirred frequently during cooling process. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded queso. 
For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU . (1.5)

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed cut cabbage, cooked onions, and cooked mushrooms above 41F in make unit. PIC stated items have been in make unit over night, no temperatures taken of items in question. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded items. (0)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) EHS observed entire speed rack of prepared foods in WIC without proper dates. PIC stated prep rack is dedicated to prepared foods to cool. PIC stated facility has had a lot of new employees and the emphasis has been date marking. CDI - PIC was allowed to date all items on speed rack due to available prep list detailing which items were prepared yesterday/today. EHS advised that if numerous items are found without dates on next inspection, items could be required to be discarded. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed ground turkey, sweet potatoes, queso, and tomato sauce cooling at inadequate rates during inspection (see temp chart). EHS did not observe active monitoring of this process for any item during inspection. Tomato sauce observed in ice bath in prep sink, however, EHS observed no active stirring. EHS observed ground turkey in deep plastic containers with plastic wrap and tight fitting lid over turkey, EHS observed queso in WIC actively cooling with no stirring. EHS provided education. EHS advised documenting cooling times, specifically when the cooling process begins to better assess cooling methods to achieve long term compliance. CDI - PIC removed all lids and plastic coverings, moved all items to WIF, and instructed food employees to actively stir. REPEAT. (1)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf EHS observed calamari thawing in three compartment sink in standing water at room temperature. PIC stated calamari has been thawing for 30 minutes. CDI - PIC removed calamari from water and placed under running water with ice. (0.5)

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. EHS observed numerous pans stacked clean with water present on interior surface of pan. EHS advised to stagger dishes to allow for adequate air drying. (0)

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. EHS observed broken tiles throughout kitchen. EHS observed grout missing between tiles throughout kitchen area. Repair. REPEAT. (1)

violations.comments:

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce above ready to eat foods. Re-organize the walk in refrigerator so that the the unwashed produce is below ready to eat foods.


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed cut melon cooling in the walk in at 50-59 F. Products were 49 to 50 F after an hour of cooling. CDI, products still had time in the 4 hour window to cool, products moved to blast freezer where they cooled to 41 F. Due to this being a repeat violation and the establishment receiving a Risk Control Plan in 2025 for cooling, the establishment is being placed back on a risk control plan. See cooling methods for additional details. 





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed feta and cheddar cheese (2 containers) in the small walk in stored above 41 F. Items were dated for today. CDI, PIC discarded the products. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed melons stored in deep covered containers cooling. Observed melons in shallow white trays stacked together and covered. Products were not cooling quickly enough after an hour. Melons moved to blast chiller and finished cooling in shallow pans. Risk Control Plan required. Establishment will develop a procedure for cooling these items and send to the EHS by 6/22 for review. A verification visit will be completed in 10 days. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance.

rows.general_comments: - Facility has undergone a name change and change of concept since the previous inspection (documented in previous visit sheets). EHS confirmed that the facilities LLC remained the same and that it is still listed as active on the Secretary of State website. Owners stayed the same, there is just a new management crew on site to run day to day operations. Changes remain cosmetic in the dining room areas with the kitchen remaining untouched with no addition or removal of equipment. Facility currently is only offering a small menu of a few grilled cheese options containing only RTE ingredients. PIC stated that they have plans of changing the menu in the future and was informed to contact the EHS prior to making changes to the menu. Facility is well equipped to serve the current contents of their menu.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. EHS observed box of raw chicken stored directly on top of a box of raw shell eggs. PIC stated they had just placed the box of chicken there earlier during prep. EHS observed raw cut beef stored over raw pork in 2-door freezer located by fish tank in dining room. These raw proteins have been cut and bagged into portion bags, then placed into freezer. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC rearranged storage order. No contamination observed. (1.5)

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed 2 mandolins, 1 immersion blender, and one pan soiled with food debris and residue stored with clean equipment. CDI - PIC removed equipment and pans to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (0)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.comments: 6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf- *Observed - No paper towels at handsink in freezer area. *CDI - PIC provided paper towels.

*REPEAT* 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- 
*Observed - Frozen Raw Chicken Breast suran wrapped on sheet tray above RTE foods in Standing RIF. *CDI - PIC reorganized RIF correcting storage order.
*Observed - Frozen plastic containers of Raw Beef and frozen plastic containers of Raw Shrimp above frozen plastic containers of cooked shrimp and cooked soup in Standing RIF. *CDI - PIC had employee reorganize Standing RIF correcting storage order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P- *Observed - Dish Machine unable to dispense chlorine sanitizer at proper concentration while being used to sanitize. *CDI - PIC cut clogged part of sanitizer tube and reconnected tube. Correcting issue and allowing dish machine to dispense sanitizer at proper concentration.


For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- 
* Observed - Shredded Beef, Sliced Pork and Steamed Pork cooked yesterday above 41F, see temp chart for details. *CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded items.
*Observed - Noodles, Sliced Tomatoes, Sliced Brisket, Cut Meatballs and cooked Tendon above 41F with little change in temperature after 40min. *CDI - PIC placed items into RIF and WIC to improve cooling rate.
PIC utilizing Large Flip Top Unit to cool cooked/reheated foods.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:

Observed doors on both dumpsters in the dumpster area open. Ensure doors are closed when the dumpster is not in use.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed metal bowls wet stacked on the clean dish rack of the facility.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed cold rail drawer unit gaskets in need of repair or replace. PIC stated that there is a work order placed for the units gaskets. Ensure that all equipment is maintained in good repair.

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.114 Drains in dumpsters and waste containers shall have drain plugs in place. Observed drain plug on right dumpster of the dumpster area with no drain plug. Ensure drain plug is replaced. 


5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors.
Observed doors on both dumpsters in the dumpster area open. Ensure doors are closed when the dumpster is not in use.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed wall joint in the rear of the WIF peeling away from the wall. Ensure that the wall unit is repaired and made to be smooth and easily cleanable.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed raw shell eggs stored over RTE sauces and spinach dip in one of the RICs. -CDI- Eggs were moved to the bottom shelf. (0 pts) 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P 
Observed chlorine sanitizer at 0ppm in the dish machine. EHS discovered that the facility was using degreaser instead of a
chlorine sanitizer. -CDI- Chlorine sanitizer was added to the dish machine and was able to reach 100ppm consistently.
REPEAT*** (3 pts) 



For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed 2 items that were incorrectly date marked or did not have a date mark. -CDI- Items were correctly date marked. (0 pts)


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-304.12 (C ) Store in-use utensils on a clean portion of the food preparation table or cooking equipment. The utensils and storage surface must be cleaned and sanitized every 4 hours.
Observed knife being stored in between 2 prep units. -CDI- Knife was removed and placed to wash. (0 pts)

violations.comments:
*REPEAT* 2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. *Observed - No CFPM onsite during time of inspection.

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing -Pf- *Observed - Fork stored in basin of handsink. *CDI - PIC removed fork from handsink.
Points deducted remain the same due to severity and improvement.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- *Observed - Raw Chicken stored above Prepped Raw Onions in WIC. *CDI - PIC corrected storage order.

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P- *Observed - Raw Chicken stored above Raw Beef in WIC. *CDI - PIC corrected storage order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf- *Observed - Robot Coupe Slicer stored clean with dried food debris build up and Cheese Grater stored clean with dried food debris. *CDI - PIC placed items in dish area to be washed, rinsed and sanitized.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. - Observed a container of raw beef stored on a shelf directly above a container of raw scallops in the WIC. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC rearranged storage order so that the raw beef was below the raw scallops.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P - Observed 2 large containers of raw chicken to be stored on a shelf directly above cases of unwashed broccoli in the WIC. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC moved raw chicken to another shelf with other raw chicken.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. - Observed a spiral slicer to be stored on the lower shelf behind the sushi counter with visible debris on the food contact surfaces. 

CDI - PIC placed at 3 compartment sink to be W/R/S.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed a large bowl containing a mixture of cream cheese and various vegetables such as carrots, onions, and celery to be in the WIC and not provided with a date mark. PIC stated the mixture was prepared the day before. REPEAT.

CDI - PIC date marked container.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19(B)(2) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Ready to eat cut or chopped TCS produce or opened TCS hermetically sealed food that begins TPHC at 70 F or less, must remain at or below 70 F during the 4 hour time as a public health control period and be marked with the discard time. - Observed the egg wash container at the wok line to be held on TPHC but was not time stamped with a start or discard time. PIC stated it was brought out at approx 10:30am.

CDI - Cookline employee time stamped container.

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. - Observed 3 vacuum sealed packages of raw tuna to be in the sushi counter lowboy refrigerator. Package thawing instructions stated “remove from packaging” and the packages observed at the time were fully intact.

CDI - Sushi employee moved to the WIC and placed on a sheet pan. Cuts were put into the packaging to remove them from the ROP environment.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. - Observed a gap at the base of the back door when closed, allowing for the potential entry of vermin into the facility. REPEAT.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. - Observed a cutting board to have large chunks missing, heavy scarring, and heavy staining which rendered it no longer easily cleanable.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the racks used in the dishwasher to have heavy accumulations of black grime. REPEAT.
- Observed a buildup of grease and grime between the fryers and the wok line at the time of the inspection. REPEAT.

violations.comments: 3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU . Observed beef being cooled in large plastic container on prep counter not in an ice bath. Beef measured 85F. PIC stated beef was cooked approximately 1.5 hours prior. Beef would not have met cooling parameters without EHS intervention. CDI: PIC separated beef into 3 shallow metal containers and placed in walk in cooler to rapidly cool.

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ . Observed chili guadillo salsa being held at 95F on stove top. PIC stated salsa is used for dipping tortillas and had been prepared 2 hours prior. CDI: PIC reheated salsa to over 165F before returning to hot hold.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P - Observed containers of grilled chicken strips, cut lettuce, and Swiss cheese to be in the cookline lowboy at temperatures of 44-49F. Items in the opentop are held using TPHC and were properly time stamped at the time of the inspection. PIC stated that staff leave the opentop open throughout lunch usually and stated that is why the lowboy is running warm. Ambient observed at 49F. PIC stated that the items have been in the lowboy since approx. 9am and were not sure how long the items have been out of temperature for. 

CDI - All out of temperature items voluntarily discarded. Ambient of the unit was observed at 41F and the lettuce moved up into the unit while the EHS was typing the report was able to hold/maintain 41F. EHS advised the staff to not keep the lid open during rushes or if they are going to do that they should not keep TCS foods in the lowboy portion as it causes temperature issues during that period.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. - Observed 2 shakers of cinnamon sugar and salt to be above the fryers and were not labeled as to their contents.

CDI - PIC labeled shakers.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. - Observed the racks of the WIC to have accumulations of food debris/grime.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. - Observed the floors beneath equipment and in the hard to reach corners to have accumulations of food debris/packaging.

rows.general_comments:
Facility was told to stop specialized processes for sous-vide braised lamb until variance letter from the state to approve of this specialized process has been received to PICs. For more information on HACCP and Variance for state of North Carolina, please refer to the links below:
1. Association of Food and Drug Officials (AFDO) HACCP templates: https://www.afdo.org/haccp-plan-review/resources/
2. U.S. Food & Drug (FDA): https://www.fda.gov/food/hazard-analysis-critical-control-point-haccp/retail-food-service-haccp
3. NCDHHS Variance Committee: https://ehs.dph.ncdhhs.gov/faf/food/correspondence.htm
4. "Managing food Safety: A Manual for the Voluntary Use of HACCP Principles for Operators of Food Service and Retail Establishments" 
http://www.fda.gov/downloads/Food/FoodSafety/RetailFoodProtection/ManagingFoodSafetyHACCPPrinciples/Operators/UCM077957.pdf

violations.comments:
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf
Observed no paper towels available at the handsink located at the front make line.
CDI: Employee refilled paper towels immediately.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pan of soft-boiled eggs (served undercooked for ramen dishes) stored on a shelf above pans of mung bean sprouts, inside the lowboy portion of make table 2.
CDI: PIC corrected storage order to prevent contamination.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed pan of blanched thin rice noodles in portioned bags temping above 41F. Date label showed that the rice noodles were prepped on 6/15. Other pans of rice noodles from same batch on the same day were temping at proper temps. Unclear what may have happened.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded rice noodles.

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed facility's online menu missing asterisk for soft-boiled egg on menu item "curry laksa." Physical menus available at the facility are correct, but is not asterisked on the online menu. Facility launched this new menu 6 weeks prior. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

violations.description: Compliance with variance, specialized process, reduced oxygen packaging criteria or HACCP plan
violations.comments:
3-502.11 Obtain a variance for specialized processes.-Pf 
Observed facility is using a sous-vide method for braised lamb (which launched on new menu 6 weeks prior). PIC showed SOPs of the process, to which EHS found gaps in the process and observed SOPs were not a true HACCP plan. PIC stated that the lamb is received raw, cooked on the premises, cut into chunks, then mixed with a sauce, before they use a vacuum-sealer to vacuum-seal the marinated lamb chunks into packages, which are then held for a shelf-life of up to 2 days, which will be placed into the sous-vide per order. Upon communication with the Head Chef via phone, it was brought to EHS's attention that the corporate office has contacted the state for a variance letter, but facility has yet to receive the approval letter. EHS explained this method requires a variance from the state prior to executing this specialized process, and PICs understood.
CDI: All 4-oz ROP'd packages of braise lamb were voluntarily discarded by the PICs and menu item was 86'd for all dine-in and carry-out options. 
**NOTE: Per permit conditions...
- The PERMIT HOLDER shall not add equipment or change the menu prior to obtaining approval from the REGULATORY AUTHORITY. A
change in the type of food establishment or food operations is required to be approved by the REGULATORY AUTHORITY to ensure
compliance with the rules.
- Plans and specifications must be submitted for review and approval before any equipment is added or removed and /or remodeling of any
part of the FOOD ESTABLISHMENT premises.
- The PERMIT HOLDER shall obtain written approval from the REGULATORY AUTHORITY prior to beginning specialized methods of food
processing.
EHS provided PICs with resources for HACCP and Variance for specialized processes.

For more information on how to apply for a variance please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed pan of black pepper bean sprouts cooling inside the WIC and not changing in temperature after 38 minutes had surpassed. Pan was made of plastic and covered with a lid.
CDI: PIC moved pan into the standing reach-in freezer to rapidly cool down.
Observed several sheet trays full of cooked white rice cooling inside the WIC and not changing in temperature after 38 minutes had surpassed. Rice was not spread out evenly on sheet trays, and left in large chunks/piles. 
CDI: PIC had food employees break up the rice into small pieces and into the reach-in freezer to rapidly cool down. 
Observed pan of blanched thin rice noodles that had surpassed the cooling parameters and holding above 41F. Pan was made of plastic and lid was covered, and portioned bags inside the pan were filled past the fill line.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded rice noodles.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed one sanitizer bucket full of QUAT sanitizer measuring 0ppm.
CDI: PIC dumped out the sanitizer bucket and refilled with properly concentrated QUAT sanitizer, measuring 200ppm.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted.
Observed stacks of metal pans and plastic pans wet-stacked and stored on the clean dish rack.
Ensure all pans are completely air-dried prior to stacking together.

rows.violation_text:
10 - Handwashing sinks supplied & accessible: 6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf
Observed no paper towels available at the handsink located at the front make line.
CDI: Employee refilled paper towels immediately.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed pan of soft-boiled eggs (served undercooked for ramen dishes) stored on a shelf above pans of mung bean sprouts, inside the lowboy portion of make table 2.
CDI: PIC corrected storage order to prevent contamination.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

20 - Proper cooling time & temperatures: 3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. 
Observed pan of blanched thin rice noodles in portioned bags temping above 41F. Date label showed that the rice noodles were prepped on 6/15. Other pans of rice noodles from same batch on the same day were temping at proper temps. Unclear what may have happened.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded rice noodles.

For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

25 - Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods: 3-603.11 (C )(2) A Consumer Advisory Reminder shall include asterisking the food item to a footnote that states: * Consuming raw or undercooked meats, poultry, seafood, shellfish, or eggs may increase your risk of foodborne illness.- Pf
Observed facility's online menu missing asterisk for soft-boiled egg on menu item "curry laksa." Physical menus available at the facility are correct, but is not asterisked on the online menu. Facility launched this new menu 6 weeks prior. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure facility is in compliance.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

29 - Compliance with variance, specialized process, reduced oxygen packaging criteria or HACCP plan: 3-502.11 Obtain a variance for specialized processes.-Pf 
Observed facility is using a sous-vide method for braised lamb (which launched on new menu 6 weeks prior). PIC showed SOPs of the process, to which EHS found gaps in the process and observed SOPs were not a true HACCP plan. PIC stated that the lamb is received raw, cooked on the premises, cut into chunks, then mixed with a sauce, before they use a vacuum-sealer to vacuum-seal the marinated lamb chunks into packages, which are then held for a shelf-life of up to 2 days, which will be placed into the sous-vide per order. Upon communication with the Head Chef via phone, it was brought to EHS's attention that the corporate office has contacted the state for a variance letter, but facility has yet to receive the approval letter. EHS explained this method requires a variance from the state prior to executing this specialized process, and PICs understood.
CDI: All 4-oz ROP'd packages of braise lamb were voluntarily discarded by the PICs and menu item was 86'd for all dine-in and carry-out options. 
**NOTE: Per permit conditions...
- The PERMIT HOLDER shall not add equipment or change the menu prior to obtaining approval from the REGULATORY AUTHORITY. A
change in the type of food establishment or food operations is required to be approved by the REGULATORY AUTHORITY to ensure
compliance with the rules.
- Plans and specifications must be submitted for review and approval before any equipment is added or removed and /or remodeling of any
part of the FOOD ESTABLISHMENT premises.
- The PERMIT HOLDER shall obtain written approval from the REGULATORY AUTHORITY prior to beginning specialized methods of food
processing.
EHS provided PICs with resources for HACCP and Variance for specialized processes.

For more information on how to apply for a variance please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed pan of black pepper bean sprouts cooling inside the WIC and not changing in temperature after 38 minutes had surpassed. Pan was made of plastic and covered with a lid.
CDI: PIC moved pan into the standing reach-in freezer to rapidly cool down.
Observed several sheet trays full of cooked white rice cooling inside the WIC and not changing in temperature after 38 minutes had surpassed. Rice was not spread out evenly on sheet trays, and left in large chunks/piles. 
CDI: PIC had food employees break up the rice into small pieces and into the reach-in freezer to rapidly cool down. 
Observed pan of blanched thin rice noodles that had surpassed the cooling parameters and holding above 41F. Pan was made of plastic and lid was covered, and portioned bags inside the pan were filled past the fill line.
CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded rice noodles.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed one sanitizer bucket full of QUAT sanitizer measuring 0ppm.
CDI: PIC dumped out the sanitizer bucket and refilled with properly concentrated QUAT sanitizer, measuring 200ppm.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted.
Observed stacks of metal pans and plastic pans wet-stacked and stored on the clean dish rack.
Ensure all pans are completely air-dried prior to stacking together.

rows.address:
