CHARLOTTE, NC 28134

violations.description: Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events
violations.comments:
2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf Observed no vomit and diarrhea procedures in the facility. CDI: EHS provided procedures to PIC and discussed procedures. 



For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P Observed employee with gloves crack raw eggs into a pan then discard gloves, then proceed to don new gloves to work with RTE food without a hand wash in between. CDI: PIC instructed employee to discard gloves and re-wash hands.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P Observed two packages of strawberries in WIC with mold present. CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded strawberries.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed deli slicer stored as clean with deli meat debris still present on equipment and blade. PIC stated that the slicer had not been used today and should be clean. CDI: PIC instructed employee to W/R/S slicer. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed cooked tomatoes, and chicken noodle soup in hot well with temperatures below 135F. PIC stated that they were placed in the steam well around 7:30. Observed low water in steam well and items double panned. PIC stated that items are not always placed on stove to reheat before being placed in steam well. CDI: PIC instructed employee to reheat items on the stove. Items reached over 165F. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed cut tomatoes, sliced cheddar cheese and sliced American cheese in fliptop with temperature above 41F. Observed a couple sheet pans of portioned unsalted butter stored on table to be used for service. Butter had a date of 6/16 and temperature was 67F. EHS explained that unsalted butter needs to be refrigerated. PIC stated that the tomatoes and cheeses were transferred from the WIC to the prep top an hour prior. CDI: PIC voluntarily discarded unsalted butter and placed cheese and tomatoes in WIC to quickly cool. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed multiple containers of cooked corn(6/9), cooked sausage(6/9), queso(6/9), sliced turkey(6/9), penne sauce(6/3), sheet pan of cooked corn(5/29) in WIC. Observed jalapeño aioli(6/9), milk(6/7) in RIC. Observed two chubs of turkey and one chub of ham without date marking. PIC stated that they were opened Saturday. CDI: PIC dated chubs of turkey and ham and voluntarily discarded items out of date. REPEAT.



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed raw chicken and chicken broth thawing in two different prep sinks with running water. Water temperature measured 81F. Items were partially thawed and began thawing an hour prior. CDI: PIC moved items to WIC to continue thawing.

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed two food employees working with food without hair restraints.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF - Observed no signed employee health agreement on site at the time of the inspection. REPEAT.

CDI - EHS provided written copy of employee health agreement and had both kitchen employees sign at the time of the inspection.

For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P - Observed the dishwasher to be testing at 0ppm of chlorine at the time of the inspection. Sanitizer bottle was observed empty at the time of the visit.

CDI - Sanitizer bottle changed, machine primed, and retested at 150ppm.

For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P - Observed a container of tater tots to be at a temperature of 96F on the counter beneath the heatlamp at the cookline. PIC stated that they make the tater tots in the oven every day and then they sit under the heatlamp until needed. EHS advised the PIC that switching to TPHC could be more beneficial since they are often only open from 7-11pm and typically sell through it on busier weekend nights.

CDI - Tater tots placed on temporary TPHC. EHS filled out TPHC procedure with PIC and explained the process to the PIC to utilize going forward.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) - Observed 2 opened packages of hot dogs in the lowboy refrigerator that were not provided with date marks of any kind. Upon questioning it was discovered they were opened the previous Wednesday, making today day 8 for the items. EHS explained how the 7 day date marking window and with the facility being closed on Monday/Tuesday the EHS advised that the kitchen staff discard all TCS items on Sunday night or first thing Wednesday morning to avoid date marking violations in the future.

CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf - Observed a container of crab rangoon dip and corn salsa to be cooling in the opentop refrigerator at the time of the inspection. Items observed at temperatures of 62F and 44F, respectively. PIC stated that they had just prepared both items within the last 30 mins. EHS explained how all cooling should be done in the WIC and following prep they should utilize the WIC for cooling. The opentop refrigerator is a unit that is not designed nor approved for rapid cooling.

CDI - Containers moved to the WIC to rapidly cool.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

EHS observed children’s cheeseburger with consumer advisory. PIC state they were not aware of the mistake on these menus but they would never serve an undercooked burger to children. PIC stated they will have new menus ordered immediately. 
CDI- PIC instructed food employee to mark out the asterisks on the menus. Facility shall notify EHS when new menus are printed for verification that asterisks was removed. 
-0-

violations.comments:
5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. -Observed handsink behind bar (serving coffee) with items stored inside. -CDI- PIC removed items to make handsink accessible.


6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf -Observed no paper towels at handsink behind bar (serving coffee). -CDI- PIC provided paper towels.

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P -Observed tomato stored in prep unit with visible blue/white growth. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the tomato.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P -Observed raw chicken and raw lamb stored above shrimp and cooked crab meat in the tall reach-in cooler. -CDI- Storage order was corrected.



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P -Observed cooked mushrooms, raw shrimp, and cooked crab meat above 41F in tall reach-in cooler. All other TCS food in tall RIC was at or below 41F. PIC stated the mushrooms, shrimp, and crab meat were originally stored in the under-grill cold drawers this morning, yet the cold drawers have not been cold holding properly recently, so the food was moved to the tall RIC after realizing the cooler was malfunctioning again. PIC stated the cold drawers underneath flat top grill have not been cold holding below 41F recently, and they are waiting on cooler parts for repair. -Do not store TCS foods in the under-grill drawers until the cooler is fully repaired and properly maintaining food at or below 41F. -PIC stated the food was moved to the tall RIC less than one hour ago, therefore PIC placed mushrooms, crab meat, and shrimp on ice in tall RIC for rapid cooling. By end of inspection, EHS observed shrimp, mushrooms, and crab meat cooling at sufficient rates to reach 41F. -Contact EHS when cooler is repaired- 3 day verification visit required-
REPEAT

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed sliced ham (6/7) and cooked carrots (6/7) held past their 7-day timeframe. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the carrots. PIC stated the date mark on the ham was incorrect and it was actually prepared yesterday, 6/16. PIC corrected date mark.


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf -Observed pork sausage, held on 4-hour TPHC, missing a time stamp for when it was removed from temperature control. -CDI- PIC verified when sausages were originally placed on TPHC (cooking completion) and properly added time stamp.

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. -Observed ROP salmon thawing within the ROP bag environment. -CDI- PIC cut hole in ROP bag of salmon.

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments:
3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf
EHS observed facility was recording the date shellstock was received not the date the last product was used. EHS observed shellstock tags were not in chronological order. 
CDI- PIC put tags in chronological order. PIC added the date the last shellstock was used to the tag as they were present when it was used. 
-0-

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). 
EHS observed the following items held beyond 7 days - cooked sweet potatoes, chicken ju, cooked carrot and garlic oil. 
CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the items. 
-1.5-

violations.comments:
2-301.14 (E) Wash hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. -P
Observed food employee drop thermometer on kitchen floor, Employee picked up thermometer and returned to food prep.
CDI - EHS intervened and Employee washed hands and donned new gloves.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed portioned RTE beef balls stored in same container as portioned raw beef inside the prep unit. 
CDI - Beef balls removed 
Observed raw beef stored above crab Rangoon in prep unit.
CDI - Storage order rearranged. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination.
Observed pink build-up on the interior of the ice machine.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed multiple items previously prepared, frozen, removed from the freezer and no date mark/label was added (Beef Broth, brown rice, & Lo Mein). The date of prep and the thaw date should follow all items prepared and thawed at a later date for service.
CDI - The proper date marks were added to each food.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container.
Observed scoop handles submerged in MSG and uncooked rice.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed chicken salad cooling while in a deep, covered pan in WIC during inspection. Observed cut lettuce cooling while in a deep, covered plastic container in WIC during inspection. Both items missed cooling window.
CDI - Both items voluntarily discarded.

violations.comments:
4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed numerous pans stored as clean with food debris or sticker residue present on equipment. EHS observed a few pans soiled with food debris and residue on interior of pan. EHS observed sticker residue on numerous pans stacked together next to ovens. CDI-PIC removed pans to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. (1.5)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. EHS observed in use wiping cloths being stored in sanitizer at 0ppm. PIC observed sanitizer dispenser empty. CDI - PIC replaced sanitizer and instructed food employee to replenish all sanitizer buckets with fresh sanitizer. (0)

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. EHS observed food residue accumulating on shelving in barista WIC. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. (0)

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. EHS observed flooring in WIC accumulating large amount of food residue and debris. WIC has concrete flooring that is unsealed, creating an area that is hard to clean. PIC has attempted to remove debris from flooring and is still currently looking to replace/reseal. EHS observed grout in flooring near dish machine missing, creating an area where water is beginning to pool in between tiles. (1) REPEAT.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed TCS food items stored inside WIC 1 cold holding above 41F. Ambient air temp of WIC 1 was at 48F, and EHS noticed WIC door was left open, with employees constantly going in and out of the unit. Facility conducts equipment temperature checks and records temperatures digitally using tablets, but WIC 1 temperatures were not taken this morning. Ambient air temp dropped to 45F after 22 minutes. Another ambient air temp was taken at 11:30AM (over an hour from the first initial ambient air temp reading) after unit was blocked off from employee use, and WIC 1 was still temping 44F-47F (immediate spike in temperature when door is opened). EHS noticed ice buildup on fan inside the unit, and heavy condensation dripping from the fan. PIC entered a work order in for technicians to come out and check on the unit. All TCS food items from WIC 1 were moved into WIC 2, as WIC 2 has plenty of storage space to hold TCS food items. PICs stated they understand to keep raw chicken stored separated from all other TCS food items inside WIC 2. 
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed heavy buildup of food scraps on interior of standing reach-in freezer. Observed soiled handles on cooking equipment and cold holding units.
Increase cleaning frequency.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments:

Observed two employee drinks stored above single service items and a prep surface.

violations.comments:

For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

*REPEAT* 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- *Observed - Pork Roll and Fish in Small “weekend” Flip Top very over stacked and above 41F and Raw Chicken Wings in Sandwich Flip Top Lowboy above 41F. *CDI - PIC placed Pork Roll and Fish into Small Flip Top Lowboy and voluntarily discarded Wings.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- *Observed - White Rice and Noodles sitting out in ambient. Of being in WIC. *CDI - PIC placed items into WIF to blast chill and speed up the cooling rate.

violations.comments:

2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-201.11 Store toxic materials to avoid contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens, and single-service/use items. -P
7-202.12(A) Use poisonous or toxic materials according to manufacturer's label. Use pesticides approved for use in commercial food service only.-P
Observed two bottles of chemicals stored above single-service items. Observed a can of “Hot Shot” Wasp spray that is not rated for commercial kitchen use. CDI - Chemicals were moved to a lower shelf and “Hotshot” spray was removed from the kitchen.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
Observed a container of salt without a label.

violations.description: Hot & cold water available; adequate pressure
violations.comments:
10 DAY VR.
5-103.11(B) Provide sufficient hot water to meet the peak hot water demands.-Pf
Observed hot water at 3 comp sink drop below 110F when other sinks or dish machine are also running. With no other water in use, hot water still does not exceed 110F. EHS will complete a water calculation and assess for the capacity of current water heater. Follow up to come. 
Note: hot water is compliant for manual ware-washing so long as no other appliances are also running. PIC must ensure the required 110F minimum for hot water is available when manual ware washing. (-0.5)

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed raw fish stored over RTE ribs in the WIC. Ensure that no raw food is stored over RTE foods. -CDI- PIC rearranged storage order so that raw was no longer over RTE. 
No Contamination observed. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf- Observed can opener with build up of residue and food debris on the cutting blade. -CDI- PIC moved to 3-Comp to be properly washed, rinsed, and sanitized. 

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P- Observed chlorine dish machine with dishes present inside. EHS ran the dish machine 3 times and after each run of the machine, the chlorine concentration was 0PPM. -CDI- Dish machine was primed using the sanitizer primer switch and then was reading at approximately 100PPM afterwards. 
Ensure that dish machine is properly primed and is maintains proper sanitizer concentrations. 



For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed coleslaw and cheese slices in the Prep Unit above 41F. PIC stated that the food was added to the unit approximately 1 hour prior to the EHS arrival. -CDI- Food was added to the WIC to properly cool, food was cooling properly by the end of the inspection. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed a tub of ambient prepared croaker fish at 50F in the fish RIC. Food employee stated that they were prepared 15 mins prior to EHSs arrival. After 30 minutes the fish were 49F. EHS notes that the fish were packed into a large bus tub. -CDI- PIC moved croaker fish into WIF to properly cool. Fish were cooling at a proper rate by the end of the inspection. 
Ensure proper cooling methods are followed. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed unlabeled water and oil squirt bottles, and an unlabeled shaker bottle. Ensure that all working containers of food are properly labeled.

EHS notes that PIC stated that the facility has commercial pest control 2 times a month. EHS states increasing the pest control in the summer months and seek out any small areas of entry. 
Ensure facility is free from pests.

violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed large Ice machine not functioning at the time of the inspection. PIC stated that the ice machine has a maintenance request in for it. 
Ensure equipment is maintained in good repair.

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

37 - Food properly labeled: original container: 3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed unlabeled water and oil squirt bottles, and an unlabeled shaker bottle. Ensure that all working containers of food are properly labeled.

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed approximately 5 pests present in the facility throughout the inspection. EHS notes that PIC stated that the facility has commercial pest control 2 times a month. EHS states increasing the pest control in the summer months and seek out any small areas of entry. 
Ensure facility is free from pests.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed large Ice machine not functioning at the time of the inspection. PIC stated that the ice machine has a maintenance request in for it. 
Ensure equipment is maintained in good repair.

rows.general_comments:
Facility is going to use standing commercial RIC cooler to hold milks for coffees until other RIC is repaired. EHS verified the unit had an ambient air temperature of 40F. 

EHS will return within 3-days to verify the other RIC is functioning properly. 

EHS reminded staff that the facility permit restricts them from cooling. EHS observed prepared rice onsite that staff stated they cooked then cooled. 
If facility has any questions regarding permit please contact EHS - EHS left business card onsite.

violations.comments:
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
EHS observed the hand sink being used a dump sink during inspection with dishes in the sink. 
CDI- EHS provided education and items were removed from the sink. 
**REPEAT** -2-

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
EHS observed the RIC at the FOH unable to maintain temperatures of 41F or below. Food in the unit ranged from 45-46F with the highest ambient temperature reaching 46F. See temperature chart. EHS spoke with staff who stated the food had been present in unit since the day prior and no temperatures were recorded for today. 
CDI- All TCS food that was 41F and above was voluntarily discarded.
**REPEAT** -3- 3 DAY VR REQUIRED. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

EHS observed visble food debris on dry storage shelf’s, more frequent cleaning is recommended. 
-0-

5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair.
EHS observed 3-compartment sink with a leak. Repair as soon as possible. 
-0-

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed cooked chicken thighs and cooked shrimp cooling at ambient temperature in kitchen. CDI - Both items were taken to walk in cooler to cool. PIC has been advised to initiate active cooling as soon as cooked TCS foods reach 135F. -0 points-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

Pf Observed bags of raw shrimp thawing at room temperature on prep sink drain board. CDI - Frozen shrimp were taken to reach in freezer to remain frozen. -0 points-

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P- Observed raw chicken stored above raw beef in the WIF both outside of the original packaging. Ensure that proper storage order procedures are followed in WIC holding always, and in holding in the freezer when food is outside of the original packaging. -CDI- EHS, PIC and Food employees discussed the violation, and PIC corrected storage order. 


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed 5 unlabeled sauces on the wok station, and observed unlabeled container of sesame seeds used on the prep line. Ensure all working containers of food are properly labeled to identify contents.

3-304.12 (C ) Store in-use utensils on a clean portion of the food preparation table or cooking equipment. The utensils and storage surface must be cleaned and sanitized every 4 hours. Observed a butcher knife stored in between crack between the prep unit and a prep surface. Ensure that all in-use utensils are stored on a clean portion of food prep tables or cooking equipment. -CDI- Knife was moved onto the clean portion of prep surface.

rows.general_comments: Permit amended 6/16/26 by A. Hall to reflect the addition of a ventless combi oven for the purpose of cooking raw sausage and raw bacon. Facility must adhere to all storage order requirements as well as ensure that all final cook temperatures are met.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed overall limited control over the kitchen by certified food protection manager. Ensure that staff are aware of cross contamination issues, allergens within products, and are monitoring temperatures. 




A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed raw bacon stored over RTE sausage in RIC. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. REPEAT VIOLATION
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed veggie omelet and turkey sausage held on counter with no temperature control below 135F. CDI: Omelets rapidly reheated to 169F. Sausage voluntarily discarded. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed items in glass front reach in above 41F. -CDI- Refrigerator turned down. Items were placed in refrigerator at beginning of the morning and monitored while cooler recovered. PIC voluntarily discarded eggs. After 45 min, items holding at 41F. Future cold holding violations will result in a risk control plan and/or permit action. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 

Observed no date mark on hard boiled eggs or RTE sausage patties in reach in cooler. CDI: Items date marked with the date opened. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
4-204.112 (B) Provide and position correctly an air thermometer in cold/hot holding equipment. Observed no air thermometer in glass reach in cooler.

3-602.11 (B) (5) Food packaged on premises for customer self service shall include a label with the major food allergens contained in the food -Pf. Observed individually wrapped muffins with no allergen labeling. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to ensure that facility has allergens listed. 
3-602.11(C) Bulk food available for consumer self-dispensing shall be prominently labeled in plain view with the manufacturer label provided with the food, or another method of identification that includes the information in 3-602.11 (B)(1), (2), and (6). Observed bulk dispense cereals where common name of the food was incorrect (Raisin Bran identified as Special K Red Berries) and the other required information was missing. CDI: Information provided as required.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-306.11 Protect food on display using shields, packaging, or other effective means. -P Observed hard boiled eggs stored in an open container for customer self service inside of reach in refrigerator. CDI: Eggs voluntarily discarded. Eggs shall not be stored in this manner without a cover. 
3-306.13(B)(C) Consumer self-service operations shall have utensils or effective dispensing methods to protect the food from contamination and be monitored by trained food employees.-Pf Observed oatmeal toppings without proper protection or self closing lids. Ensure that staff are adequately monitoring toppings and/or proper protection is provided. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 10 days to assess food protection. 
3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed employee’s hot tea stored on top of boxes of pancake mix. CDI: Tea relocated to designated area.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floor in dry storage area in need of sweeping. Observed dust build up on ceiling tiles adjacent to AC vent. 
6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed 1 absorbent ceiling tile in kitchen.

violations.comments:

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments: 3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf- Observed no shellstock tag for opened container of Chesapeake oysters. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the oysters. -REPEAT-

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P -Observed all TCS foods in WIC above 41F (blanched fries, chicken wings, cooked potatoes, mornay sauce, curry sauce, sundried tomatoes, grilled corn, veal demi, chicken demi, blue cheese, ranch, duck fat, charred tomatoes, duck meat, crab claws, crab cakes, green goddess dressing, chipotle cream cheese spread, cut cabbage, cooked mac noodles, mozzarella, chimichurri, picatta, heavy cream, cooked beets, bacon jam, beef tallow). Observed ambient temperature of WIC above 41F. -PIC voluntarily discarded all items listed above. PIC contacted cooler technician during inspection. PIC adjusted temperature of WIC during inspection and, by end of inspection, EHS observed ambient temperature of WIC drop to 39F (PIC stated temperature dial was adjusted to low 30’s). -Ensure WIC is repaired and maintains proper cold holding temperature at or below 41F- 3 day verification visit required-



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf- 
*Observed - Only Flip Top Unit full of TCS food items above 41F, unit was unplugged overnight, WIC with TCS Foods and an ambient temperature above proper cold holding temperature of 41F. See temp chart for details. Daily temperatures shall be taken to ensure temperature controlling units are operating properly. This way staff or management can catch issues so that units are repaired quickly and TCS Foods are maintaining 41F or less to ensure safety for consumers.

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

*REPEAT* 2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. *Observed - No CFPM onsite during time of inspection.

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

*REPEAT* 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- *Observed - All cold holding units at establishment in disrepair and all TCS foods within above 41F, see temp chart for details. PIC and Chef voluntarily denatured with Bleach and discarded all TCS food items.
A verification visit will be conducted within 72 Hours to ensure units have been repaired and are able to maintain proper Cold Holding temperatures.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
*Observed - Cheese Sauce, Taco Meat and Milk in WIC without date marking. All items in Flip Top Unit without date marking. *CDI - All items were voluntarily discarded due to cold holding violation.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: *REPEAT* 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. *Observed - Grease build up on hood system and around fryers in hard to reach areas.

54 - Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. *Observed - No Lids on dumpster during inspection. Replace/repair dumpster to provide lids.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: *REPEAT* 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. *Observed - Ceiling tiles missing in dry storage area and floor tiles missing under fryers.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed egg rolls, small food container of fried chicken, and small container of lo mein in walk in cooler with cold holding temperature between 47F and 50F. Walk in cooler had an ambient air temperature of 50F. Walk in cooler holds mostly non-TCS whole vegetables and packages of uncooked noodles. All TCS foods are stored in reach in and flip top coolers at prep line. All raw proteins are stored in walk in freezer. CDI - TCS foods were discarded. PIC has called technician for repair. A return visit will be conducted to ensure compliance. -REPEAT- -1.5 points-

violations.comments:
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) -Observed cooked crawfish, roasted red peppers, sherry remoulade, cooked shrimp, and pineapple salsa without date marks. -CDI- PIC verified the prep dates for all items by speaking with the chef and properly added date marks.

violations.comments:
3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed numerous items in top of make unit and reach in of make unit (pizza unit) above 41F (see temp chart). PIC stated temperatures had not yet been taken for the day, but has not observed any fluctuations with temperature. PIC stated during the summer kitchen temperature increases which could lead to malfunctions. EHS observed ambient temperature above 41F (see temp chart), after thirty minutes no change in temperature was observed. EHS has advised to have unit assessed. PIC voluntarily discarded all TCS items stored in unit overnight or beyond 4 hours; all other items placed in unit within four hours were removed and placed into WIC to cool. PIC will place pizza toppings stored in top portion on TPHC until unit is fixed and will not store TCS items in reach in compartment. EHS will provide TPHC form and return within 3-days to assess repairs. (1.5)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(C ) Single Service and Single Use items shall be kept in the original protective package or stored using other means that protect the item until used. EHS observed open pizza to-go boxes stored in hallway near restrooms and accessible to guests. The hallway is monitored by camera and to-go boxes are elevated off the ground. However, during the last inspection EHS stated boxes must remain in original package or covered (if opened) when stored in hallway. CDI - PIC removed boxes to wrap with plastic wrap prior to storing in hallway. (0.5) REPEAT.

violations.comments:
2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P. OBSERVED EMPLOYEE wash hands for about 5 seconds and turn the water off without using paper towel. EDUCATION wash hands provided on hand washing procedures.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf EHS observed inadequate cooling rates for quinoa salad. EHS observed item actively cooling in make unit with flip top lid open during entire inspection. EHS observed containers of quinoa salad in WIC covered and actively cooling. EHS observed inadequate rates during inspection. CDI - PIC removed lids from quinoa salad and replaced container in make unit with container observed below 41F. **EHS is notating an improvement on procedures observed. EHS observed numerous items actively cooling during inspection with proper cooling rates observed and efficient methods exhibited. EHS will de-escalate points due to improvement. EHS will deduct full points on next inspection if improvement does not continue. Last inspection EHS observed 1 item inadequately cooling vs. 1 item that was observed cooling properly. This inspection EHS observed 1 item with inadequate cooling rate vs 4 items observed with adequate rates. No risk control plan required at this time. (0.5) REPEAT. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. 
Observed a pan of marinated raw chicken skewers stored on a shelf above a pan of marinated raw red snapper, stored inside RIC 3.
CDI: PIC corrected storage order and EHS provided food storage order handout to serve as a visual aid.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed multiple TCS food items stored inside the lowboy cooler above 41F (see temp chart). Ambient air temp of the cold holding unit was 45F. PIC stated food items are not kept inside this unit overnight, and are pulled from the WIC in the morning. Food items have been stored inside this unit less than 4 hours. PIC moved all TCS food items into the RIF to rapidly cool down. PIC contacted technician to have cold holding unit checked for repairs. EHS checked ambient air temp again after PIC had manually adjusted temperature dial on the unit, and ambient air temp of the unit was now temping at 47F.
VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days to ensure facility is in compliance.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed pan of paneer without a date label, stored inside the make table. PIC stated this was prepared yesterday.
CDI: Date label created.
Observed bottle of salad dressing (house-made w/TCS ingredients) without a date label, stored inside the lowboy cooler. PIC stated this was prepared on Sunday.
CDI: Date label created.
Observed pan of boiled potatoes and a pan of cut cabbage both missing date labels, stored inside the lowboy cooler. PIC stated these were prepared on Sunday as well.
CDI: Date label created. 
Observed opened jug of whole milk missing date label. PIC stated they opened this jug of milk on Sunday.
CDI: Date label created.
REPEAT VIOLATION.
*Note: Although a repeat violation, EHS not deducting full points today as facility has now established a date-marking system since previous inspection, and it appears some TCS food items were missed. PICs understood which food items require date labels and which do not, and understand that food items are not held past 7 days from prep.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water.
Observed ROP'd packages of raw salmon fillets thawing under running water, temping above 32F (no longer in the frozen state), without slits in the packaging.
EHS explained to PICs food item is required to be removed from the ROP'd packaging prior to thawing, per manufacturer's label to prevent biological contamination.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
Observed a large bin full of various spices/seasonings used at the make line, unlabeled. Observed all bottles of different sauces/oils/vinegars used at the make line unlabeled.
Ensure all working containers of food items are labeled for easy identification.
REPEAT VIOLATION.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed scoops used inside rolling bins of sugar and rice stored with their handles commingled within the non-TCS food products.
CDI: PIC removed scoops from the bin to prevent contamination.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted.
Observed stacks of metal pans wet-stacked and stored at the clean dish rack.
Ensure pans are completely air-dried prior to stacking together.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed heavily damaged gasket on left bottom door of standing 4-door reach-in cooler at the make line. 
Repair damaged gasket to ensure cold holding unit is maintaining proper temperatures.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed heavy oil accumulation on the hood system. Observed heavily soiled gasket and interior non-food contact surfaces of the lowboy cooler. Observed soiled interior non-food contact surfaces of RIC 2. 
Increase cleaning frequency.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. Observed no certified food protection manager on site during inspection. 






For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed turkeys cooling in open air grab and go cooler with approximately 1 hour left to cool to 41F still at 60F. CDI: Products placed in the walk in cooler to rapidly cool. 





For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed no date marking for items in prep top cooler. CDI: PIC prepared the items and was able to date mark them for when she prepared them. Observed ready to eat ham portions date marked for 10 days in the reach in cooler. Items were not held past 7 days today. VERIFICATION REQUIRED within 3 days to ensure ready to eat items are held for 7 no more than 7 days from date of preparation. Observed no date marks on items pulled from frozen to indicate initial prep date or thaw date. Items were marked with discard date for 3 mos from when frozen only. CDI: Items marked to reflect the remaining number of days to be held. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed items prepared today such as glazed turkey, slider sandwiches, potato salad, and chicken salad cooling in grab and go case; potato salad and ham salad cooling in reach in part of prep top, and sliced ham and sliced tomatoes cooling in prep unit. -CDI- Items in prep top cooler cooled at proper rates rise. EHS educated PIC on proper cooling methods and items in the grab and go cooler were relocated to the walk in cooler and properly cooled. REPEAT VIOLATION

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed no lid on chest freezer under counter. Only non-TCS desserts in freezer however items were not frozen to the touch. PIC indicated during the holidays TCS items are stored here. Ensure that TCS items in this unit remain at 41F and below and unit is used correctly. Observed shelving chipping/peeling and rust on the shelves used in dry storage.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed interior and exterior surface of prep unit in need of cleaning. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. Observed some build up on shelf where microwave is stored. Observed significant ice build up in all 3 chest freezers. Ensure units are defrosted regularly. Observed utensil holder at dirty part of sink with significant build up. Ensure holder is cleaned regularly to prevent build up. Observed spill under cabinet near drink machine. REPEAT VIOLATION

21 - Proper hot holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- *Observed - Fried Rice below 135F in HH Rice Warmer. *CDI - PIC had employee reheat item to 165F and place item back into Rice Warmer on TPHC. PIC labeled item from 2:20PM-6:20PM.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
*Observed - Noodles prepped yesterday without date marking. *CDI - PIC date marked items.
*Observed - Diced Pork without date marking in Left Flip Top Unit. PIC stated item was grabbed from bulk container stored and date marked in WIC. *CDI - PIC date marked Pork in Flip Top to match Batch in WIC.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: *REPEAT* 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. *Observed - Multiple dry goods with plastic To-Go containers stored inside used as scoops. Remove and replace with scoops that have handles.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. *Observed - A couple of Metal Containers Wet Stacked stored clean on clean dish rack.

violations.comments:
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf.
Observed PIC fill up a sanitizer bucket with chlorine and water, obtaining water straight from a handsink.
CDI: EHS reminded PIC to utilize handsinks only for handwashing.

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed all TCS food items (fried chicken, crab rangoon, cut cabbage, lomein noodles, cooked beef, bbq pork, fried tofu, boiled shrimp, hard-boiled eggs, re-hydrated rice noodles, pooled eggs) stored inside both make tables that bore no date labels. Food employee stated all above mentioned food items were prepared yesterday, but they had not gotten a chance to add the date labels this morning.
CDI: Food employee created date labels for all above mentioned TCS food items.


For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed one unlabeled spray bottle stored at the 3-compartment sink. PIC stated the pink liquid inside the spray bottle is grill cleaner.
CDI: PIC created label for spray bottle.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta.
Observed pans of seasonings and sauces stored on the rolling cart at the make line not labeled.
Ensure all working containers of food are labeled for easy identification.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed soiled gaskets in cold holding units throughout facility. Observed soiled interior surfaces of make tables and standing reach-in cooler.
Increase cleaning frequency.

violations.comments:
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display.-P. 
Observed raw chicken stored above raw ground beef and steak in WIC.
CDI- No contamination observed, chicken moved to bottom rack. (-0)

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-303.12(B)(C)(D) Unpackaged food may not be stored in direct contact with undrained ice except: whole raw fruits or vegetables; cut raw vegetables such as celery, carrots, or potatoes; tofu; and raw poultry and raw fish received immersed in ice.
Observed shredded lettuce stored in large amount of water in WIC. EHS advises PIC not to store TCS produce floating in water. (-1)

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized.
Observed multiple cutting boards in dish storage area with deep scoring, no longer cleanable. 
CDI- PIC removes boards from service and to place an order for more today, 06/15. (-0.5)

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture.
Observed unsealed wooden floor panel at exit/entry to WIC which is subject to large amounts of water during cleaning and shows signs of deterioration. Remove old wooden floor attachment and replace with a smooth and cleanable material which quickly dries. (-0.5)

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair.
Observed damaged siding at wall behind cook line hand sink. Repair so wall is not subject to entry of water and is cleanable.

rows.violation_text:

Observed raw chicken stored above raw ground beef and steak in WIC.
CDI- No contamination observed, chicken moved to bottom rack. (-0)

Observed shredded lettuce stored in large amount of water in WIC. EHS advises PIC not to store TCS produce floating in water. (-1)

rows.address:
6914 MATTHEWS MINT HILL RD STE 100
 
MINT HILL, NC 28227
rows.general_comments: Facility added a WIF to the rear interior of the kitchen. The WIF is not in use yet but will be soon. EHS spoke with EHS Mark Oliver. EHS Oliver stated to have the PIC of the facility to call him prior to the use of the WIF. PIC understood.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed raw chorizo stored over top of corn in the stand up RIC and several raw foods out of the original packaging in the Stand Up RIF stored over top of RTE foods. -CDI- PIC rearranged storage order. 
Ensure raw animal foods are not stored over RTE foods. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed cooked onions and cooked onions in peppers in the HH Cabinet below 135F. When EHS asked when they were cooked and placed into the Cabinet PIC and food employees determined it was over 2 hours prior. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed rice in the WIC above 41F. Observed all TCS foods in the grill drawers above 41F. Ambient reading of the unit was 55F. Foods included raw beef, chicken, shrimp, RTE Al Pastor, and cut tomatoes. -CDI- All foods above 41F were voluntarily discarded. PIC called a refrigerator tech during the inspection. EHS will return to ensure that grill drawers is properly repaired. 
-3-DAY VR- 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed facility with several foods past date in the prep unit in both the flip and lowboy portion. PIC explained that foods were frozen on the original date. Foods did not have a second date once taken from the freezer. -CDI- Foods were able to be back dated to when they were pulled from the freezer. All foods were within the 7 day window. 
Ensure proper date marking procedures for when RTE foods are thawed for use. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-501.13 (E ) Reduced oxygen packaged fish, required to be kept frozen until time of use per labeling, shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing under refrigeration or upon completion of thawing using running water. Observed white fish in ROP original packaging thawing in the WIC. -CDI- EHS coached the PICs and food staff that when ROP fish is thawed the ROP package must be slit or the fish must be removed during the thawing process.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed 3 oil bottles unlabeled present near the cook line of the facility. 
Ensure that all working containers of food are properly labeled.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed a scoop present in seasoning with its handle resting atop food. Ensure that all scoops are stored so that their handles are not in direct contact with food.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed 3 heavily scored cutting boards. PIC stated that they would resurface or discard and replace the cutting boards. 


4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed grill drawer reading at an ambient temperature of 55F. External thermometer showed unit as 34F ambient. Ensure that grill drawer unit is repaired.

violations.comments: 2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P Observed employee wash hands and recontaminate hands by turning off faucet without a barrier. CDI hands rewashed.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed soiled strainer, plates stored at top of dry storage rack with food build up, and bowl with leftover food debris. CDI items moved to sink for cleaning. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed no dates on items in walk in unit that was prepared the day before with dates on them. CDI items dated. Observed pans of Mac and cheese that were in the reach in freezer that were not date marked. CDI items dated. 

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed Escovitch that was prepared 5/27 that has heat treated plant food. PIC stated the vinegar allows the to keep for longer than 7 days. Told PIC they must maintain 41F or below unless they want to apply for Variance to render the product nonTCS. CDI product discarded. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. Observed no written procedures that were prepared for plantains held on TPHC. CDI written procedures provided. 

3-501.19 (C ) (3) Follow written procedures for cold foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 6 hours. Food shall be marked with start and discard time. -Pf Observed plantains with no time stamps once time started. CDI PIC provided prefilled out 2 hour time stamps for the day. Told PIC they can not prefill time stamps. Time stamps must be recorded as items are removed from temperature control. CDI time stamps fixed.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
Observed pans of cabbage, rice, curry chicken, goat meat, and rice on rack sitting out for 30 minutes and items are only dropping 2 degrees. CDI items moved to walk in cooler. Repeat. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed racks and shelving in need of cleaning and bottom of reach in freezer at the beginning of inspection with soiled area at the bottom of the unit and interior of containers to hold knives and ice scoop that was soiled with debris. Repeat.

violations.description: Required records available: shellstock tags, parasite destruction
violations.comments: 3-203.12 Retain tag on the bag of shellstock until empty. Record date on the tag when the last shellstock from the container is used. Store tags in chronological order for 90 days. -Pf -Observed one container of clams in the cold wells on the make line without proper shellstock tag. - PIC located tag in WIC of clams from the same batch. PIC stated facility has implemented ‘A, B, C’ system to identify batches of clams, so that employees can place some clams on the make line during the day, yet keep the larger container of clams with the tag in the WIC. PIC stated the employees yesterday did not add the ‘A, B, C’ identification onto the clams on the make line. EHS advised creating a written SOP and conducting a training to ensure all employees are aware of this shellstock identification system. -CDI-PIC added identification letter to this smaller batch of clams. -REPEAT-

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
-Observed mixtas rice and azafran rice in the cold wells on the make line without date marks. -CDI- PIC referenced larger batches of both rices in WIC and properly added date marks to the containers.
-Observed proscuitto in WIC without date mark. -CDI- PIC was present yesterday when prosciutto was opened and properly added yesterday’s date.
-Observed portioned containers of ground beef and shredded beef in the low reach-in cooler on the make line held past its 7-day timeframe (cook date of 6/8). Observed container of pork in WIC held past its 7-day timeframe (cook date of 6/6). -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the ground beef, shredded beef, and pork.
-REPEAT- If same violation is observed on next inspection and no improvement is noted, facility may be placed on Risk Control Plan for date marking. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
TPHC procedure template may be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety
3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P Observed several TPHC hot items that had been on hot line without time stamp. CDI Employee started placing time on log while EHS was on line.

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf. Observed tcs food items thawing under running water that was 80F at prep sink. Cool running water at or below 70F needs to be used for thawing. CDI- PIC put ice into the thawing container to bring water temperature to 70F or below.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-901.11(A) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils shall not be used until air dried or adequately drained before contact with food. Observed several clean pans and containers stacked together while still wet. Ensure items are dried before stacking.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed the cutting board at the deli station is excessively scored and no longer easily cleanable. It needs resurfaced or replaced.
5-205.15 Maintain a plumbing system in good repair. Observed faucet in coffee station in front that needed to be sealed into place in the sink.
