violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A) Employees shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and other items needing protection can not result. Observed employee on grill dip hand into expired pico and eat it on the line above prep area. CDI- Discarded.

2-301.14 Wash hands before engaging in food preparation including working with exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and unwrapped single service and single use articles. -P Observed employee handle soiled wishes, then go to get clean ones without hand wash. Observed Employee handle soiled equipment then go to handle food without hand wash. CDI- Hands washed. 
2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food.- P Observed employee handling soiled dishes, touch mouth and complete other tasks inside and outside of the kitchen, Then put gloves on to prepare food without washing hands.

3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P Observed employee wipe gloved hands on pants, then go to prepare food. CDI- Gloves changed and hands washed.

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed piles of plates with old food debris. CDI- Dishes taken to be cleaned. Observed slicer stored clean with old food debris. CDI- Slicer taken to be cleaned.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed food in prep top unit holding above 41F. All items were in the 50Fs. CDI- All items discarded. Unit is not holding above 41F. This is the 6th consecutive time facility has been out on cold holding. EHS highly recommends completing a risk control plan. EHS left copy of the risk control plan. ITS issued. Facility has not keep checking temps and getting corrective actions. PIC knew that the unit was not holding above 41F and had called to get the unit serviced. Unit has been used for day while non-functional. 3 DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED.

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed sliced turkey and marinara without date. rows.general_comments:
NOTE: EHS observed cooked items cooling in tall reach in units (Paneer, eggs). Observed several other foods cooked and cooled in the facility that were not agreed upon by EHS and PIC. Facility agreed to only cook and cool onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb. EHS and PIC agreed to adding rice to the cooling list. EHS observed eggs, rice, chicken, paneer, goat soup, dal, potatoes, veg mix among other items cooked and cooled. EHS found several issues with cooling today. Cooked items were cooled in the tall reach in unit. Several other items were cooling with lids on and wrapped in the tall reach in. NO temperatures were taken throughout the cooling period. The facility places the items in the tall reach ins to cool, then leaves them until needed. This process is not approved. Facility will be restricted to cooking and cooling (rice, onion sauce, tomato sauce, lamb and goat). 
If EHS finds other items cooked and cooled without prior approval an INTENT TO SUSPEND WILL BE ISSUED. 

NOTE: Disinfectant wipes used for prep tables do not specify that they are food safe. Directed use does not include food contact surfaces. Use bleach sanitizer or food safe sanitizer for cleaning. Kitchen.

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed several food items cooling with lids on. Cdi- Removed. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

EHS reiterated to PIC during the last visit that facility is limited to cooling to 4 items (onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb). EHS observed cooked and cooled vegetables, dal, goat soup, cauliflower, paneer, rice, biryani, marinated chicken, chicken biryani among other things. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf Observed chicken thawing in prep sink without running water. CDI- Water ran on chicken.
3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed several spice containers without labels.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed live roaches in kitchen area. EHS reviewed most recent pest control log. Pest control came out 2/24. Continue use pest control services, clean frequently and keep food covered.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-304.13 Linens and napkins may not be used in contact with food unless used to line a container for the service of foods and are replaced each time the container is refilled for a new customer. Observed napkins stored in container of food in tall reach in.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean dishes stored at prep sink with raw chicken. Observed clean knives stored on soiled magnet strip. CDI- All items taken to be cleaned. 

4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed several dishes wet stacked in clean dish storage area.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed tall reach in freezer gasket heavily damaged. Observed broken handle on rice cooker. Repair/ replace

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tall reach in units, squeeze bottles in need of cleaning. Pts not escalated due to improvement

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floors in need of cleaning, under and around equipment. Pts de-escalated based on improvement. Observed wall behind 3 comp in need of cleaning.

rows.violation_text:
1 - PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties: 2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed violations pertaining to reheating, hot holding, date marking, storage order, clean dishes, cross contamination among other things.

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

6 - Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed employee drinks stored throughout the kitchen area.

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw shrimp stored over ready to eat items in the low reach in unit. Observed Opened raw chicken stored above opened beef in tall reach in freezer. Freezer was at 44F. Items were thawed. Raw chicken/ meat juice covered over the bottom of the cooler. CDI- Items relocated to proper order.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

16 - Food-contact surfaces: cleaned & sanitized: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed cleaned dishes splattered with raw chicken juice. Observed robo coupe stored clean, soiled with old food debris. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

19 - Proper reheating procedures for hot holding: 3-403.11 (A),(D) Reheat all TCS foods to 165F within 2 hours if food is to be hot held until service. -P Observed pre-cooked rice reheated to 120F. CDI- Reheated to 165F.

20 - Proper cooling time & temperatures: 3-501.14(B) Quickly cool TCS foods that are prepared from room temperature ingredients. Food must be to 41F within 4 hours. -P. Observed food cooling above 41F after 4 hours of cooling. CDI- Discarded


For more information, please visit: www.meckncgov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P6x8b1ZVOxU .

21 - Proper hot holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed eggs, chick peas, potato soup and onion/ tomato sauce mix holding below 135F in hot well. CDI- All items reheated to 165F.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed coconut milk and unsalted butter in prep top unit holding above 41F. CDI- Coconut milk discarded. Butter placed in low reach in. Pts de-escalated based on improvement. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed goat soup, dal and beans without date marking. CDI- Items dated. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed several food items cooling with lids on. Cdi- Removed. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

4-301.11 Provide equipment in number and capacity so that cooling, heating, and holding temperatures are achieved. -Pf Observed facility without enough equipment to sufficiently and safely cool items. EHS reiterated to PIC during the last visit that facility is limited to cooling to 4 items (onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb). EHS observed cooked and cooled vegetables, dal, goat soup, cauliflower, paneer, rice, biryani, marinated chicken, chicken biryani among other things. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS

Pf Observed chicken thawing in prep sink without running water. CDI- Water ran on chicken.

EHS reviewed most recent pest control log. Pest control came out 2/24. Continue use pest control services, clean frequently and keep food covered.

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-304.13 Linens and napkins may not be used in contact with food unless used to line a container for the service of foods and are replaced each time the container is refilled for a new customer. Observed napkins stored in container of food in tall reach in.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean dishes stored at prep sink with raw chicken. Observed clean knives stored on soiled magnet strip. CDI- All items taken to be cleaned. 

4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed several dishes wet stacked in clean dish storage area.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed tall reach in freezer gasket heavily damaged. Observed broken handle on rice cooker. Repair/ replace

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tall reach in units, squeeze bottles in need of cleaning. Pts not escalated due to improvement

Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floors in need of cleaning, under and around equipment. Pts de-escalated based on improvement. violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-102.11 (A) - (C )(1), (4)-(16) The Person in charge shall demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this code by (A) Complying with the Food Code by having no violations of Priority Items during the current inspection, (B) Being a certified food protection manager, or (C) by responding correctly to the inspector's questions related to the operation of the food establishment. -Pf. -Observed multiple violations/repeat violations (cooling parameters) present during inspection. Observed no certified food protection manager onsite. Observed PIC unaware of proper cold holding temperature or cooling rates. Observed PIC unable to locate thermometer, sanitizer, or sanitizer test strips. Ensure PIC is knowledgeable of food safety procedures, the Food Code, and foodborne disease prevention. -10 day verification visit required-




Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. -Observed no certified food protection manager on site during the inspection. -REPEAT-


For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf -Observed no soap at either handsink during inspection. -CDI- Soap was provided at sinks.
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf -Observed no paper towel at front handsink during inspection. -CDI- PIC provided paper towels.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. -Observed mac and cheese not meet cooling parameters after cooling overnight in RIC. Observed mac and cheese at 44F, while PIC stated it was cooked yesterday and has not been removed from cooler since. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the mac and cheese. -REPEAT-


For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) -Observed mac and cheese cooked yesterday without a date mark. -CDI- PIC verified prep date of item properly added date mark. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf 
-Observed mac and cheese miss cooling parameters after cooling overnight in RIC. Observed mac and cheese covered with metal lid, still at 44F while cooling in a tall reach-in cooler overnight. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the mac and cheese. 
-Observed multiple pans of jerk chicken cooling in RIC, covered with tin foil and stacked on top of each other. -CDI- PIC un-stacked pans and uncovered them to allow for maximum air circulation. -When cooling foods, leave uncovered to quickly cool. 
REPEAT


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. -Observed multiple container of flour/sugar without a label. Label all containers with the common name of the food. -REPEAT-

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. -Observed few flies throughout kitchen.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. -Observed visibly soiled wiping cloth stored directly on table top surface. -CDI- PIC moved wiping cloth to 100ppm chlorine sanitizer solution.
REPEAT

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. -Observed torn gaskets in tall RICS. Observed broken door on tall RIC, preventing door from properly closing. Repair gaskets and door. -REPEAT- 

4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent. -Observed non commercial grade chest freezers. Observed no commercial grade rice cooker. They are in good working order. Will not deduct points for freezers. When freezer is replaced, replace with commercial grade freezer going forward. -REPEAT-

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments: 4-303.11 (A) (B) Cleaning agents and chemical sanitizers that are used to clean and sanitize equipment and utensils shall be provided and available for use during all hours of operation.-Pf -Observed no sanitizer available at facility at start of inspection. -CDI- During inspection, PIC obtained chlorine/bleach sanitizer.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. -Observed food buildup and residue on front and handles of equipment and shelving. Observed dark residue buildup on dry storage containers (for sugar, flour). -REPEAT- Increase frequency of cleaning.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.115 Discard items and litter stored in the waste areas and enclosures as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance. -Observed excessive amount of unused equipment and items in back alley, directly outside back door. Remove old equipment and waste in back alley and in hallway to prevent the area from becoming a pest harborage. -REPEAT-

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. -Observed several torn, cracked missing tiles throughout kitchen. Observed wall by the back door chipped and in need of repair or replacement so that it’s easily cleanable. -REPEAT- 

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. -Observed dark buildup on floors and walls throughout facility. Increase frequency of cleaning.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. - Observed personal employee food stored in RIC above customer food. Store employee food on separate, lower shelf to prevent contamination of customer food. -CDI- PIC relocated employee food to bottom shelf. -REPEAT-

rows.general_comments: EHS provided PIC with county office number to call for re-inspection. EHS reviewed inspection in detail and advised to reach for any needed help prior to requesting re-inspection.

violations.comments:
2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. EHS observed no CFPM during inspection. (1)

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

2-301.15 Only wash hands in handwashing sink. -Pf EHS intervened during inspection and requested food employee to wash hands after contaminating gloved hands by touching back door. EHS observed food employee go to prep sink and begin washing hands with gloves still on. EHS instructed food employee to remove gloves and wash hands in a hand sink. CDI - Food employee properly washed hands in hand sink. 

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P EHS observed employee washing hands after intervention without using soap or removing gloves. EHS provided education on handwashing procedure. CDI - Food employee washed hands properly. (2)



For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

6-301.11 Provide soap for handwashing at each handsink. -Pf EHS observed no soap at hand sink by back prep sink. CDI - PIC supplied hand soap to hand sink. 

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf EHS observed paper towels missing by two out of three hand sinks. CDI - PIC supplied paper towels to both hand sinks. 


5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. EHS observed hand sink obstructed by prep table in kitchen area. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC moved prep table out of the way of the hand sink. (1)

3-201.11(C) Packaged food shall be labeled as specified in Law (e.g. Identify food name, specific ingredients, manufacturer name and address, nutrition information, labeling in English. - Pf EHS observed two bags of powder and a container of paste in dry storage area with no English labeling. EHS advised that any item purchased through a vendor shall bear a legible label in English. CDI - PIC removed items from facility and will reach out to supplier for more information on items. (0)

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P EHS observed numerous raw proteins in WIC being stored over RTE foods. EHS educated PIC on proper storage order. CDI - PIC rearranged storage to prevent contamination. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. EHS observed various different proteins being slacked in WIC in the same container. These proteins were individually bagged but placed all in the same container. These proteins included raw chicken, beef, and pork. EHS observed no contamination, items were still in the thawing process. CDI - PIC rearranged storage order. REPEAT. (3)
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. EHS observed pans stacked by dish machine stored as clean with residue present on outside of pans and sticker residue on numerous pans. EHS observed deli slicer soiled with food residue on back of blade; PIC stated deli slicer has not been used since Sunday. CDI - PIC removed pans and deli slicer and placed at three compartment sink to be wash, rinsed, and sanitized. REPEAT. (3)

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P EHS observed white rice in rice cooker at 112F. PIC stated it was made earlier this morning and possibly wasn’t set at the right temperature for hot holding. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded rice. (0)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P EHS observed an oil mixture made from reducing spring onions in oil. This item is held at room temperature. EHS advised that since the oil contains cooked vegetables it is deemed TCS and should be dated marked and temperature controlled. EHS discussed potential use of TPHC and reviewed process with PIC. CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded oil mixture. (0)

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). EHS observed no date marking system in place during inspection to ensure items are being monitored and held for 7 days or less. EHS observed only a few items with dates on them, however, the dates were from prior products. PIC was able to identify items that PIC had prepared the day prior but some items could not be determined on when they were made. No prep list available to verify. EHS allowed facility to date mark items the PIC stated he made the day prior. All other items were voluntarily discarded. EHS provided education on date marking and will return in ten days to observe compliance and ensure a system has been established for long term compliance. (1.5)

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf EHS observed bottle of cleaner store on the floor under hand sink. PIC unable to determine what was inside bottle. CDI - EHS voluntarily discarded chemical. (0)

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. EHS observed numerous sauces and seasonings on wok line without proper labels. EHS advised working containers of food that is not easily identifiable should have a legible label identifying what the item is. REPEAT. (2)

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments:
3-305.14 Unpackaged food shall be protected from contamination during preparation. EHS observed raw fish being prepared on drain board of three compartment sink at the same time a food employee was rinsing a mop in the middle compartment. EHS advised that facility cannot prepare items at three compartment sink unless sink compartments have been washed, rinsed, and sanitized and the compartments are free of any soiled equipment and utensils. CDI - PIC instructed food employee to utilize prep sink for fish preparation. (0)

3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. EHS observed boxes of dry goods on the floor in dry storage area. EHS observed raw proteins stored in containers on the floor in WIF. EHS advised to move all food off the ground at least six inches. (0)

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. EHS observed interior of make units accumulating food debris and residue. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. (0)

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. EHS observed ceiling tile above can wash breaking apart and exposing the interior insulation of tile. EHS advised faciltiy to replace tiling. 

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. EHS observed AC vents and ceiling tiles throughout kitchen soiled with food residue and dust. EHS observed food debris on walls around make units and wok area. Increase cleaning frequency to avoid accumulation. REPEAT. (1)

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. EHS observed food for employees stored in WIC unlabeled and not in a designated area. EHS advised that food for employee use only should be segregated from food for service and labeled to identify items for employee use. (0)

2 - Certified Food Protection Manager: 2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. EHS observed no CFPM during inspection. (1)

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

8 - Hands clean & properly washed: 2-301.15 Only wash hands in handwashing sink. -Pf EHS intervened during inspection and requested food employee to wash hands after contaminating gloved hands by touching back door. EHS observed food employee go to prep sink and begin washing hands with gloves still on. EHS instructed food employee to remove gloves and wash hands in a hand sink. CDI - Food employee properly washed hands in hand sink. 

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P EHS observed employee washing hands after intervention without using soap or removing gloves. EHS provided education on handwashing procedure. CDI - Food employee washed hands properly. (2)



For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf EHS observed paper towels missing by two out of three hand sinks. CDI - PIC supplied paper towels to both hand sinks. 


5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. EHS observed hand sink obstructed by prep table in kitchen area. EHS provided education. CDI - PIC moved prep table out of the way of the hand sink. (1)

11 - Food obtained from approved source: 3-201.11(C) Packaged food shall be labeled as specified in Law (e.g. Identify food name, specific ingredients, manufacturer name and address, nutrition information, labeling in English. - Pf EHS observed two bags of powder and a container of paste in dry storage area with no English labeling. EHS advised that any item purchased through a vendor shall bear a legible label in English. CDI - PIC removed items from facility and will reach out to supplier for more information on items. (0)

15 - Food separated & protected: 3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P EHS observed numerous raw proteins in WIC being stored over RTE foods. EHS educated PIC on proper storage order. CDI - PIC rearranged storage to prevent contamination. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. EHS observed various different proteins being slacked in WIC in the same container. These proteins were individually bagged but placed all in the same container. These proteins included raw chicken, beef, and pork. EHS observed no contamination, items were still in the thawing process. CDI - PIC rearranged storage order. REPEAT. (3)
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

EHS observed deli slicer soiled with food residue on back of blade; PIC stated deli slicer has not been used since Sunday. CDI - PIC removed pans and deli slicer and placed at three compartment sink to be wash, rinsed, and sanitized. REPEAT. (3)

rows.general_comments:
EHS had thorough conversation with PIC about cooling and cooling methods. PIC was under the impression that food had to be left out cooling in ambient air until it reached 70F. EHS educated PIC that food shouldn’t be left out for that much time in ambient air and best practice is to not place it in cold holding units immediately after removing from cooking to ensure that equipment is not damaged/that other items in unit are not affected by adding very hot items in cold holding units. EHS recommended leaving items out at ambient air on ice baths for 30-45 minutes and then placing them in WIC or RIF to facilitate rapid cooling. EHS educated PIC of cooling parameters and that cooling does not start until items reach 135F. EHS recommended facility monitor cooling more closely and place time stamps on cooling items (once cooling starts) along with temperatures. EHS educated PIC that the best way to determine how long items can be left out at ambient air is by closely monitoring the process and checking temperatures throughout. EHS educated PIC on other methods such as separating into smaller containers and active stirring. Facility has purchased ice wands. EHS educated PIC on leaving ice wands in the large sauce containers once placed in WIC, after removing from ambient air. 

For re-inspection call 980-314-1620

violations.comments:
2-301.15 Only wash hands in handwashing sink. -Pf- Observed on food employee wash hands at prep sink. -CDI- EHS educated food employee on where to wash and food employee washed hands at appropriate sink. 
2-301.12 (A),(B) (C) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel) and use disposable paper towels or similar clean barriers when touching faucet handles to avoid recontaminating hands. -P- Observed two food employees wash hands for less than 20 seconds and not use a barrier to turn off the faucet, and one did not use soap. -CDI- EHS educated both on proper handwashing techniques and both properly rewashed hands. 
For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M . **REPEAT** -4-
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P-/2-301.14 (H) Wash hands before donning gloves to initiate a task that involves working with food. -P- Observed food employee leave out back door, lock door, remove gloves and begin to put on a clean pair of gloves without washing hands. -CDI- EHS educated food employee on when to wash and food employee washed hands.
6-301.12 Provide paper towels or approved alternative for hand drying at each handsink. -Pf- Observed no paper towels at handsink. -CDI- PIC replenished paper towel supply. **REPEAT** -2-

4-602.11 (A) (4) Food contact surface of a thermometer shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, before using or storing. -P.- Observed food employee begin to use thermometer without cleaning before use and begin to store thermometer without cleaning after use. -CDI- EHS intervened, educated food employee on cleaning thermometer before and after use and food employee properly cleaned thermometer. -0-
4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. Observed dark buildup inside of ice machine. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended.

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P.- Observed refried beans cooling for more than 2 hours above 70F. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items. **REPEAT** -1.5- **POINTS NOT ESCALATED DUE TO IMPROVEMENT** 
For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed no date marks on frozen cooked foods, milk, half and half and items in prep unit such as mozzarella cheese and queso fresco. Observed housemade salsas at FOH in sliding display cooler with no date marks. Observed items improperly marked with month “12”, per PIC, month should have been 2. Per PIC, items with no date marks had been prepared in last 1-2 days. -CDI- PIC properly date marked items. **REPEAT** -1.5-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers. -Pf- Observed spray bottle with no label. -CDI- PIC properly labeled items. -0-

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed refried beans that missed cooling parameter. Items were found cooling in large container, densely packed, with no active stirring and with a lid. Observed pupusas and carnitas cooling densely packed containers in WIC with lids. Observed birria meat cooling in ice bath in densely packed container. -CDI- EHS educated PIC on proper cooling methods and lids were removed and items separated into smaller containers to facilitate rapid cooling. Items that had missed cooling parameter were voluntarily discarded. **REPEAT** -1-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .
2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Observed food employee engaging in food prep with bracelet. **REPEAT** -1-

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed in-use wiping cloths in container with no chlorine and one less than 50 ppm. -CDI- PIC corrected sanitizer concentration to 50ppm. **REPEAT** -1-
3-304.14(E) Store sanitizer containers used for wet wiping cloths off the floor and to prevent contamination of food, equipment, and utensils. Observed sanitizer bucket stored on floor. -CDI- PIC relocated container.

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed tong handle stored in raw chicken, touching chicken. Observed scoops stored on top of rice used to scoop rice out with no handles. -CDI- EHS educated PIC on using items with handles and ensuring handles are not touching food. -0-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacking and some standing water in clean pots. -0-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving throughout. -0-

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed shelving in need of cleaning throughout. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. **REPEAT** -0.5- **OBSERVED SOME IMPROVEMENT WITH CLEANLINESS**

41 - Wiping cloths: properly used & stored: 3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed in-use wiping cloths in container with no chlorine and one less than 50 ppm. -CDI- PIC corrected sanitizer concentration to 50ppm. **REPEAT** -1-
3-304.14(E) Store sanitizer containers used for wet wiping cloths off the floor and to prevent contamination of food, equipment, and utensils. Observed sanitizer bucket stored on floor. -CDI- PIC relocated container.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container. Observed tong handle stored in raw chicken, touching chicken. Observed scoops stored on top of rice used to scoop rice out with no handles. -CDI- EHS educated PIC on using items with handles and ensuring handles are not touching food. -0-

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacking and some standing water in clean pots. -0-

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rusted shelving throughout. -0-

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed shelving in need of cleaning throughout. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. **REPEAT** -0.5- **OBSERVED SOME IMPROVEMENT WITH CLEANLINESS**

rows.general_comments:
Facility is not permitted to cool in reach in unit. As stated on the permit “ This FOOD ESTABLISHMENT is approved for cooling hot foods using approved methods and equipment. If cooling cannot be conducted safely, it will be prohibited until further evaluation occurs. COOLING MUST BE CONDUCTED IN WALK IN COOLER”. Facility is only permitted to cool in the walk in cooler. 


Facility shall designate an area to be used to for the air drying of dishes. Currently facility does not have a dedicated space and employee was observed cloth drying dishes during inspection which is not permitted. 

For reinspection call main office- 980-314-1620

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed that facility does not have an employee health policy. When asked PIC stated that employee are not trained on health policies through a written agreement or official training. EHS provided PIC with a copy of employee health policy. 10-day VR Required. -1-

For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - PObserved food employee handle raw chicken, discard gloves, and don new gloves without hands washing and attempt to handle ready to eat samosas. CDI- EHS intervened and provided education. Food employee discarded gloves and washed hands. 
2-301.12(B)(5) FOOD EMPLOYEES shall use the following cleaning procedure in the order stated to clean their hands and exposed portions of their arms, including surrogate prosthetic devices for hands and arms (5) Immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough drying using a method as specified under § 6-301.12. P Observed food employee use a rag that was located on top of a microwave to dry hands which is not an approved drying method according to 6-301.12.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw proteins stored over ready to eat foods in the walk in cooler. Observed unwashed produce stored above Rte foods in the WIC. CDI- PIC corrected storage order. **REPEAT** -1.5- 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed multiple pans with sticker residue present, dishes were stacked so that residue is touching the food contact surface. EHS observed multiple dishes stored as clean with visible food residue present. CDI- Dishes were taken to dish are to be w/r/s. -0- 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.14(A) Quickly cool cooked foods within 2 hours from 135F to 70F; and within a total of 6 hours from 135F to 41F. -P. Observed kadhai sauce cooling in two door reach in cooler. PIC stated that it cooling started at 9:30AM. At 12:30PM EHS observed the sauce at 75F. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the sauce. SEE COMMENTS -1.5-
For more information please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed tiki sauce and unsalted butter being held hot on the stove below 135F. See temperature chart. CDI- PIC heated tiki sauce up to 165F and placed butter on TPHC. **REPEAT** -1.5- 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (C)TCS food combined with additional ingredients or portions of other TCS food shall retain the date marking on the earliest/first prepared TCS food ingredient. -Pf. Observed multiple items throughout the facility without date marks present (red chutney, green chutney, potato Marsala, Marsala, Dahl, pannier X2) Facility had the following items held beyond 7 days (onion sauce 02/09-02-15 and pannier). EHS observed Dahl sauce date marked with a prep date of 02/26 when inspection occurred on 02/24. PIC stated they were present when items was prepared yesterday and that they were mislabeled. EHS advised that facility establish a procedure for date marking. CDI- EHS allowed PIC to date items as they were present on day of prep. If date marking is found to be out of compliance during further inspections facility will not be permitted to add dates, facility will be required to discard all food with missing or incorrect date marks. **REPEAT** -3-

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf. EHS observed TPHC foods time stamped from 9:30AM to 2:00PM. Time should reflect 9:30AM to 1:30PM. CDI- EHS allowed facility to correct time stamp and explained 4 hour holding period. -0-

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-207.11 (B) Store labeled, employee medications to prevent contamination of food, equipment, and other food service related items. -P Observed employee medication stored in dry storage room above food used for the facility. CDI- Medication was relocated to approved area. -0-

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf Observed large 5 gallon container cooling kadhai sauce in the two door RIC. EHS did not observe active stirring or monitoring of food. When asked if anything in the facility was cooling at the start of inspection, PIC did not mention this item. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the sauce. -0-

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-501.13 (A), (B), (C) Use approved thawing methods for frozen food such as in a cooler at 41F or below, completely submerged under cold running water according to the Code, or as a part of the cooking process. Pf. Observed samosas thawing on side of grill. PIC stated that they thaw them on the counter for service. CDI- Samosa's were still at 41F or below so EHS allowed PIC to move the item to the grill drawers for cold holding. -0-m

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed several food items stored on the floor inside of the walk in cooler, walk in freezer, on cook line and dry storage room. Maintain items at least 6 inches off of the floor at all times to prevent against contamination. **REPEAT** -2-

3-304.12 (A) In-use utensils in TCS food, must be stored with handles above the food and top of the food container.
3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
EHS observed several to-go containers with no handles being used to scoop various food items. Maintain these items out side of the container at all times. Be sure to utilize scoops with handles to prevent against potential contamination. -1- **REPEAT** 

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed hot water utensil holder at room temperature. CDI - Water was changed and placed onto stove to heat to at least 135F. - 1- **REPEAT**

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-901.11(A) Air dry equipment and utensils after cleaning and sanitizing. Observed a few containers in need of air drying before stacking. **REPEAT** -0.5- 
4-901.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils may not be cloth dried. Observed employee cloth drying dishes after they came out of the dish machine. CDI- EHS had employee run dishes through the machine again and leave them to air dry. SEE COMMENTS.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed a large amount of ice build up in WIF, appearing to be from a leak. Repair. -0-

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments: 4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf Observed facility did not have chlorine test strips for dish machine or sink and surface test strips. 10-day VR.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed low grout in areas throughout kitchen. -0-

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee belonging stored with facility food in dry storage area. -0-
