rows.general_comments: NOTE: Roaches found on food contact surfaces and prep surfaces. EHS observed severe roach infestation during visit. IMMEDIATE SUSPENSION ISSUED.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Observed issues with cold holding, hot holding, storage order, bare hand contact, hand washing. CDI- EHS reviewed all issues with PIC.

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure.-PF Observed employees without knowledge of employee health policy. CDI- PIC trained on site and signed policy. 




For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

2-301.14 (G) Wash hands when switching between working with raw food and working with Ready to Eat Food. - P Observed employee crack raw shelled eggs, then go to handle ready to eat items. CDI- Employee changed gloves and washed hands.

violations.description: No bare hand contact with RTE foods or pre- approved alternate procedure properly followed
violations.comments:
3-301.11(B) Do not contact exposed ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use suitable utensils, single-use gloves or dispensing equipment. -P Observed PIC grab RTE tomato garnish with bare hands. CDI- Discarded. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link: Handwashing and No Barehand Contact https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed spoon in handwashing sink. CDI- Removed. Pts de-escalated based on severity. Observed employee rinsing towel in handwashing sink.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Observed raw containers of chicken stored above RTE items in tall reach in. Chicken placed on bottom. 
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed vegetables stored above ready to eat items in tall reach in. 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed Knives soiled with old food debris. Observed roach crawling on clean dishes. CDI- Items taken back to be cleaned. Observed clean dishes stored in the mop sink. Dishes filled the entire mop sink area. EHS asked where they are dumping the water. PIC stated in the prep sink. DO NOT POUR MOP WATER IN PREP SINK. CDI- Removed and taken to wash. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed rice holding below 135F. CDI- Reheated to 167F. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed potatoes and veg mix holding above 41F in tall reach in. Observed cut tomato, heavy cream and coconut milk held out on prep table. CDI- All items discarded. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed several food items made days prior without date mark. Verification required within 10 days. Observed veg mix dated 2/10 with potatoes that were made 2/9. Veg mix dated for 2/9. Date food with the earliest made food items. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-202.12(A) Use poisonous or toxic materials according to manufacturer's label. Use pesticides approved for use in commercial food service only.-P Observed household pesticides in kitchen. EHS observed large roach presence. PIC paid for pest control but has no records. CDI- Pesticides removed

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments: 4-301.11 Provide equipment in number and capacity so that cooling, heating, and holding temperatures are achieved. -Pf Observed facility without enough equipment to sufficiently and safely cool items. EHS discussed with PIC during the last visit to limit cooling to 4 items (onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb). Facility was also to have a freezer dedicated to cooling only. Facility is to dedicate a freezer to cooling ONLY. Nothing else is to be in freezer. EHS observed the dedicated freezer full of pre-made frozen items and raw meats. Items are not cooled, but purchased and stored. EHS observed cooked and cooled biryani, potatoes, chicken, vegetables and other items in reach in coolers. Parameters listed above were discussed during previous visit on 2/6/26 and re-iterates today. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS. Failure to comply with permit conditions regarding proper cooling will result in permit action.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed spice, flour containers without labels throughout. Keep items labeled.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed large roach presence at facility. EHS observed roach crawling on cleaned pans, under microwave, in light fixtures and on walls. Observed roach at 3 comp sink on drainboard with clean dishes. Work to repair and eliminate any pest harborage areas, including removing unnecessary equipment and relocating items to empty shelving to keep equipment and utensils off of the floor. 
6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed dead roaches under microwave and throughout facility and hard to reach areas, such as under equipment and around water sources. 
AT EASE PEST CONTROL COMPANY

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed food stored on the floor.

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed water used for utensil storage at 94F. CDI- Water dumped.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean dishes stored with soiled dishes at 3 comp sink.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed gasket heavily torn on single door tall reach in freezer.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed flour bins, sauce bottles, tall reach in unit shelves, prep tables and prep shelves in need of cleaning.

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed cove base pulling from wall under prep sink. 
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floors under and around equipment in need of cleaning.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed personal items stored customer items throughout.

Observed roach crawling on clean dishes. CDI- Items taken back to be cleaned. Observed clean dishes stored in the mop sink. Dishes filled the entire mop sink area. EHS asked where they are dumping the water. PIC stated in the prep sink. DO NOT POUR MOP WATER IN PREP SINK. CDI- Removed and taken to wash. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

21 - Proper hot holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed rice holding below 135F. CDI- Reheated to 167F. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

22 - Proper cold holding temperatures: 3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed potatoes and veg mix holding above 41F in tall reach in. Observed cut tomato, heavy cream and coconut milk held out on prep table. CDI- All items discarded. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

23 - Proper date marking & disposition: 3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) Observed several food items made days prior without date mark. Verification required within 10 days. Observed veg mix dated 2/10 with potatoes that were made 2/9. Veg mix dated for 2/9. Date food with the earliest made food items. 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

28 - Toxic substances properly identified stored & used: 7-202.12(A) Use poisonous or toxic materials according to manufacturer's label. Use pesticides approved for use in commercial food service only.-P Observed household pesticides in kitchen. EHS observed large roach presence. PIC paid for pest control but has no records. CDI- Pesticides removed

33 - Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control: 4-301.11 Provide equipment in number and capacity so that cooling, heating, and holding temperatures are achieved. -Pf Observed facility without enough equipment to sufficiently and safely cool items. EHS discussed with PIC during the last visit to limit cooling to 4 items (onion sauce, tomato sauce, goat and lamb). Facility was also to have a freezer dedicated to cooling only. Facility is to dedicate a freezer to cooling ONLY. Nothing else is to be in freezer. EHS observed the dedicated freezer full of pre-made frozen items and raw meats. Items are not cooled, but purchased and stored. EHS observed cooked and cooled biryani, potatoes, chicken, vegetables and other items in reach in coolers. Parameters listed above were discussed during previous visit on 2/6/26 and re-iterates today. VERIFICATION REQUIRED WITHIN 10 DAYS. Failure to comply with permit conditions regarding proper cooling will result in permit action.

37 - Food properly labeled: original container: 3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed spice, flour containers without labels throughout. Keep items labeled.

38 - Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed large roach presence at facility. EHS observed roach crawling on cleaned pans, under microwave, in light fixtures and on walls. Observed roach at 3 comp sink on drainboard with clean dishes. Work to repair and eliminate any pest harborage areas, including removing unnecessary equipment and relocating items to empty shelving to keep equipment and utensils off of the floor. 
6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed dead roaches under microwave and throughout facility and hard to reach areas, such as under equipment and around water sources. 
AT EASE PEST CONTROL COMPANY

39 - Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed food stored on the floor.

43 - In-use utensils: properly stored: 3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed water used for utensil storage at 94F. CDI- Water dumped.

44 - Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled: 4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean dishes stored with soiled dishes at 3 comp sink.

47 - Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used: 4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed gasket heavily torn on single door tall reach in freezer.

49 - Non-food contact surfaces clean: 4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed flour bins, sauce bottles, tall reach in unit shelves, prep tables and prep shelves in need of cleaning.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed cove base pulling from wall under prep sink. 
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed floors under and around equipment in need of cleaning.

56 - Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed personal items stored customer items throughout.

rows.address:
4500 N TRYON ST SUITE C
 
CHARLOTTE, NC 28213
rows.general_comments: Establishment has voluntarily closed and must repair walk-in cooler before reopening.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf Walk-in cooler was holding TCS foods at 47-48F, no date marking for any TCS RTE foods present, and raw animal foods stored above RTE foods during inspection. PIC must have active managerial control and ensure employees follow rules for food safety. CDI - PIC educated on active managerial control and duties of PIC. REPEAT

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-102.12 (A) PIC shall demonstrate knowledge by being a certified food protection manager. No employee present with active ANSI food protection manager certification. REPEAT

For more information on becoming a certified food protection manager please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Raw eggs stored on shelf above fully cooked pork lard in upright cooler. Raw pork, raw beef and raw eggs stored above RTE sauces in other upright cooler. Do not store raw animal foods above ready-to-eat foods. CDI - Food storage order corrected during inspection. REPEAT

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Deli slicer stored as clean but had meat residue behind the blade. Can opener had heavy buildup on the blade and needed to be cleaned. Food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils shall be cleaned to sight and touch. CDI - Employee moved items to 3-compartment sink to be washed. Kept at half credit as small food-contact surfaces were clean unlike previous inspection. REPEAT

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Par-cooked duck in walk-in cooler measured 48F and raw pork measured 48F. Air temperature in walk-in cooler was 44F. TCS foods in cold holding must measure 41F or less. CDI - Establishment has voluntarily closed and par-cooked duck was discarded. Raw pork was moved to freezer. Establishment can’t operate without a functioning walk-in cooler. 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). No TCS RTE food in establishment was labeled with prep date. TCS RTE foods must be labeled with prep date, and used or discarded within 7 days of preparation. CDI - PIC placed prep date labels on items. Establishment is placed under risk control plan. Once establishment fixes walk-in cooler and reopens, EHS will visit establishment once per week to check date marking. If establishment can’t follow risk control plan permit action will be taken. REPEAT

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Some dead bugs observed throughout kitchen. Vast improvement from last inspection as no live roaches were observed during inspection. 0 pts.

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Food employee wearing watch. Food employees must not wear jewelry on their hands or wrists. 0 pts.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Heavily rusted shelves in walk-in cooler, walk-in freezer door has components that are corroded/rusted, hot and holding cabinet used to store ducks in cracked and held closed by tape. Repair/replace all listed equipment. // 4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E)
Resistant to damage. Establishment was using cardboard as lids for steamed buns baskets. Discard these lids. They are not washable. REPEAT

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Heavy cleaning needed throughout on cooking equipment, shelves, inside refrigeration, etc. Clean more frequently. REPEAT

6-501.114 Discard items and litter stored on the premises as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance. Large amount of old, broken equipment that needs to be removed from establishment. REPEAT // 6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Floor tile damage near 3-compartment sink. Standing water on floor in walk-in cooler. REPEAT

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf - Observed employees not knowlegable regarding handwashing and glove use. CDI by instruction. 

A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.
2-102.11 (A) - (C )(1), (4)-(16) The Person in charge shall demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this code by (A) Complying with the Food Code by having no violations of Priority Items during the current inspection, (B) Being a certified food protection manager, or (C) by responding correctly to the inspector's questions related to the operation of the food establishment. -Pf. 
Observed PIC not able to answer questions regarding food safety and process for establishment. CDI by instruction. 



Please visit our resource page, www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety, for more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety.

2-301.14 (F) Wash hands during food preparation, as often as necessary to remove soil and contamination, and to prevent cross contamination when changing tasks. -P - Observed 2 employees pick items up off ground then change gloves but not wash hands between glove and task changes including proceeding to food preparation and handling clean equipment. CDI, employee rehashed hands. 
2-301.14 (I) Wash hands after engaging in other activities that contaminate hands. -P - Observed food employee use hands to turn faucet off after handwashing.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed raw tuna stored over RTE food such as cooked chicken in walk in cooler and RTE food in salad flip top. CDI, foods relocated for proper storage. 

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-602.12 (A) The Food Contact surfaces of cooking and baking equipment shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours. Observed microwaves accumulating heavy soil and dried food debris. 

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. 
Observed several spoons, plates stored as clean but had food debris and soil. CDI, equipment relocated for cleaning. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

4-501.114 Maintain sanitizer at correct concentrations when being used to sanitize. -P 
Observed dish machine not providing required concentration of chlorine sanitizer. Unit testing 0ppm chlorine. EcoLab contacted and calibrated dish machine while on site. 


For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg and How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed a pan of house made buffalo dip with cooked chicken with incorrect date per staff of 1/20. CDI, dip voluntarily discarded. 


For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B) (1) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall have an initial temperature of 41 F when removed from cold holding temperature control or 135 F or greater when removed from hot holding temperature control. -P - Observed TPHC procedures form for ranch dressing but time not recorded on label and ranch was at 56 degrees F. CDI, time recorded per procedure. Repeat.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments: 7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf - observed 2 bottles of cleaner and a container of disinfectant not labeled. Operator unsure what was in 1 and dumped out. Disinfectant used for walls per employee labeled.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed containers of oil and several shaker containers of spices not labeled by common name.
3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses. Observed wet wiping cloth on clean drainboard of 3 compartment sink.

6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed heavy grease accumulating on walls near fryer and floors in corners of kitchen and back door. Increased general cleaning needed in hard to reach areas.

55 - Physical facilities installed, maintained & clean: 6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed heavy grease accumulating on walls near fryer and floors in corners of kitchen and back door. Increased general cleaning needed in hard to reach areas.

violations.description: Proper eating, tasting, drinking or tobacco use
violations.comments: 2-401.11 (A). Employee beverages must be stored in a designated area where contamination of food, clean equipment, utensils, linens, and single-use articles cannot occur. Observed employee bottled water on prep top.

2-301.14 Wash hands before engaging in food preparation including working with exposed food, clean equipment and utensils, and unwrapped single service and single use articles. -P. Observed staff not wash hands after returning from outside the kitchen before handling food and containers of food. CDI-staff told to wash hands.

2-301.12 (A),(B) Wash hands for at least 20 seconds. Follow the cleaning procedure to adequately wash your hands. Use warm water, soap, scrub 10 to 15 seconds, rinse, and immediately follow the cleaning procedure with thorough hand drying method (ex. Disposable Paper Towel). -P Observed staff turn off water after wetting hands, turn it back on after lathering up and turn it back off with hand. CDI-PIC discussed with staff, hands rewashed



For more information on handwashing, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training video at the following web link, https://youtu.be/BH6CLGrH10M .

5-205.11 (A) A handwashing sink shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for employee use Pf. Observed one hand sink (front) had a pitcher in it and another (dish pit) had food scraps. Hand sinks are only for washing hands, use prep sink for acquiring water for food and for rinsing food, cleaning vegetables, etc. CDI-pitcher removed
5-205.11 (B) A handwashing sink may not be used for purposes other than handwashing Pf. Observed staff get cup of water from hand sink to use for food. CDI-EHS redirected staff

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P. Observed container of moldy stuffed peppers in reach in. CDI-discarded

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P Repeat- Observed one flat of shell eggs stored above vegetable/herbs in WIC. CDI-herbs relocated to a different shelf. No escalation of point due to it being 1 item.

For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-304.15(A) Discard gloves after a task is complete or any time they are damaged or soiled. -P. Observed staff wearing gloves into bathroom and dumpster area without removing gloves before or after entering kitchen. CDI-discussion with PIC and EHS intervention both times

4-602.11 (A) (5) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, at any time during the when contamination may have occurred. P. Repeat-Observed active dishwashing with no sanitizer in the 3rd compartment. Spray bottle with undiluted bleach on clean drain board. CDI-sink cleaned and sanitized then refilled with sanitizer that tested properly. Spray bottle relocated to dirty drain board.




For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed hot foods left sitting on counter instead of putting in hot box. PIC said that while they are in lunch rush they keep it accessible then after put it in hot holding unit. Hot foods must maintain 135F or more at all times, heating back up before service is not sufficient. CDI-Discussion with PIC, one chicken dish discarded one reheated. Checked on reheating, was reheated, per staff, and now 100F. Discarded.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed milk, unsalted butter, grilled onions, sliced tomatoes, sitting on counter throughout inspection. Do not get so many items out of the cooler at once, so partially prepped food remains on counter, if get too busy put foods back into cooler. If ghee is going to be made with butter it needs to be unsalted butter or margarine so it doesn’t have to be held at 41F or below. Grilled onions were said to have been cooked 2-3 days previously and held on speed rack at room temp. Cooked plant foods must be held hot (>135F) or cold (<45F) after cooking. CDI-onions and butter discarded. Other items put back into cooler.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). Observed grilled onions, chicken gravy without date marks. CDI-both discarded.

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.comments: 7-204.11 Provide sanitizer at correct concentrations, diluting as required.-P. Observed spray bottle labeled sanitizer is undiluted bleach. CDI-discarded
2-303.11 Remove jewelry on hands and arms while preparing food. A plain ring, such as a wedding band, is allowed. Repeat- Observed food prep staff wearing several bracelets.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Repeat-Observed plastic single use sauce cups being stored in spice mixes and peanuts in main kitchen area as means to dispense product. CDI - PIC removed. EHS coached on utilizing dispensing utensils with handles.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-901.11(A) Air dry equipment and utensils after cleaning and sanitizing.
Repeat-Observed plastic hotel pans, bowls being stored as clean while wet stacked in dish pit area. Ensure all equipment is properly dried before stacking together.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.115 Discard items and litter stored in the waste areas and enclosures as they are not necessary to operation and maintenance. Repeat-Observed trash on ground around dumpster. 5 gallon buckets with old food debris and filled with water. Ground sludgy around used oil container

rows.general_comments:
Facility has PInk salt present, PIC states that the menu item that uses pink salt is no longer on the menu.
Please call the main office to request a re-inspection.

violations.comments:
3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P 
Observed raw flounder stored above RTE country ham inside the WIC.
CDI - Storage order rearranged.




For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(2) Separate types of raw animal foods from each other such as beef, fish, lamb, pork, and poultry during storage, preparation, holding, and display. -P. 
Observed ground beef stored over whole intact beef inside the WIC.
CDI - storage order rearranged.


For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P 
Observed Multiple TCS foods (eggs, shrimp stock, cooked cauliflower, Lettuce, egg salad, sliced tomatoes, buttermilk ranch, golden beets, whipped cream, pasta) stored inside Prep top 1 and 2 and also the LB RIC below the grill.
Cold holding has been out in 4 consecutive inspections. Facility will be placed on a risk control plan which requires weekly on-site visits. EHS will provide documentation via email.
3 day verification visit required.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C) 
Observed au novla sauce, romesco, garlic and oil, chimichurri, egg salad, cream held longer than 7 days or marked with the incorrect date mark.
CDI - PIC voluntarily discarded each item.
Date marking has been out of compliance for 3 consecutive inspections, if item remains out of compliance the facility may be placed on a Risk control plan or possible Permit action.
 



For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .
3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). 
Observed multiple containers of open liquid eggs with no date marking. CDI - Proper date mark added.



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments:
3-501.19 (A) (1) Written procedures for Time as a Public Health Control shall be prepared in advance, maintained in the establishment, and made available for the Health Department upon request. Written TPHC procedures must specify methods of compliance with 3-501.19 (B) or (C). -Pf. 
Observed unsalted butter held on 4 hour TPHC, Unsalted butter not listed on TPHC form.
CDI - Added to the TPHC form.

3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. Pf
Observed unsalted butter held on 4 hour TPHC with no time stamp. 
CDI - Butter held from 10 AM - 2 PM, time stamp added.

violations.description: Consumer advisory provided for raw/ undercooked foods
violations.comments:
3-603.11 (B)(2) Consumer Advisory Disclosures that are asterisked to a footnote shall state, * Items are served raw or undercooked, or contain (or may contain) raw or undercooked ingredients. -Pf
Consumer advisory omitted disclosure stating that “Items are served raw or undercooked”
10 day Verification visit required.

violations.description: Toxic substances properly identified stored & used
violations.comments:
7-102.11 Label working containers of toxic materials such as cleaners and sanitizers.-Pf
Observed 3 spray bottles of different chemical cleaners with no label throughout the kitchen.
10 day VR required.

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed no labeling on multiple containers of salt, pepper and multiple seasonings.

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils.
Observed food employee preparing salads with no hair restraints.
CDI - Employee put on hat.

3-304.14(B) Hold in-use wiping cloths in properly concentrated sanitizer between uses.
Observed wiping cloth stored in sanitizer bucket at 100 PPM of QUAT sanitizer.

3-304.12 (B) In-use utensils for food that is not TCS must be stored with their handles above the top of the food within containers or equipment that can be closed.
Observed handless scoops submerged in multiple sugars and seasoning throughout the kitchen.
CDI - Scoops removed.

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-901.11(A) Air dry equipment and utensils after cleaning and sanitizing. Observed multiple containers wet stacked on clean dish rack. Ensure containers are air dried prior to stacking.
4-903.12 Do not store cleaned equipment, utensils, linen in toilet rooms; garbage rooms; mechanical rooms; under sewer lines; under open stairwells; or under other sources of contamination.
Observed clean lids and clean kitchen-aid stored under sewer lines of hand sink near the back door.

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-202.11 Multiuse food contact surfaces shall be smooth, free of open seams, cracks, chips, pits, sharp internal angles, crevices and assembled to allow access to clean. -Pf
Observed chipped and burnt spatulas stored in clean dish area.
CDI - PIC Voluntary discard.

4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair.
Observed torn/damaged gasket in LB RIC below grill.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-302.14 Provide a test kit that accurately measures sanitizer concentrations. -Pf
Facility has QUAT sanitizer, no QUAT test strips available.
10 day VR required.

violations.description: Toilet facilities: properly constructed, supplied & cleaned
violations.comments:
5-501.17 Provide a covered waste bin in female restrooms.
Observed no covered waste bin in employee restroom.

rows.general_comments:
10 DAY VR IS REQUIRED FOR FACILITY
VRS ARE NOTED ON INSPECTION REPORT

violations.comments: 2-301.14 (F) Wash hands during food preparation, as often as necessary to remove soil and contamination, and to prevent cross contamination when changing tasks. -P- Observed employee handling raw meat at the cook line. Employee was smashing raw burger balls into pattys. Food employee then proceeded to plating RTE salmon, and prepping salad. EHS intervened to ensure that food employee took off gloves and washed hands. EHS coached food employee that glove changes and hand washes are required to prevent contamination and cross contamination when changing tasks. -CDI- Food employee removed gloves and washed hands.

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed raw fish over RTE cabbage in WIC. Observed raw bacon over multiple RTE foods on speed rack in WIC including cooked chicken gizzards. Observed Philly meat over onion rings in stand up RIF. Ensure that proper storage order procedures are followed throughout the facility. -CDI- PIC rearranged cold hold units not following proper storage order. 



For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf- Observed heavy buildup present on the wall mounted potato slicer present in the facility. Ensure that food contact surfaces are maintained as clean to sight in touch when not in use. 
10-DAY VR 
Break down and clean potato slicer

4-602.11 (E )Clean the equipment and utensils used with nonTCS foods as required to avoid contamination. 
Observed build up of gray and black soil present on the interior portion of the ice machine. Ensure that the ice machine is clean and maintained as clean to avoid any contamination. 

For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P- Observed several foods in the HH cabinet of the facility holding below 135F, foods included Mac and cheese, Chicken thighs, and chicken wings. PIC stated that the food had been in the cabinet for 4 hours. PIC stated that no temperatures have been taken to monitor the food during this 4 hours window. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded out of temp foods. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed tomatoes, lettuce, hard boiled eggs and sliced cheese out of temperature in prep top unit. Prep unit had an ambient reading of 40F. Food employee stated that food had been in prep unit since 9:00 AM this morning. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded all out of temperature food. 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D). EHS observed no date marking system put in place for the date marking of TCS food in the facility. EHS observed almost all TCS foods requiring date marking in the facility without any. -CDI- EHS went through all food items requiring date marking with PIC to determine date of cook or prep. PIC then back dated all foods requiring date marking. 
FACILITY MUST ESTABLISH DATE MARKING PROCEDURES
-10 DAY VR- 



For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

violations.description: Proper cooling methods used; adequate equipment for temperature control
violations.comments:
3-501.15 Quickly cool foods. Use methods such as open/vented shallow pans, large ice baths and active stirring. Cold air must flow around product to remove the heat. -Pf- Observed covered and stacked cabbage and beef gravy cooling at to slow of a rate in the WIC. The cooling rate for the cabbage was 0.22 and the cooling rate on the beef gravy was 0.33. Observed covered and stacked Mac and Cheese cooling in the WIC. Only one temperature was taken of Mac and cheese before PIC decided to move Mac and cheese to WIF to cool. 
-CDI- PIC moved both the beef gravy and the cabbage into the WIF to cool. PIC also removed the lid on both containers of food. 
Ensure that proper cooling methods are used to ensure cooling food reaches proper temperatures in the proper amount of time. 




For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Cooling Food Safely https://youtu.be/P6x8b1ZVOxU .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed several spice shakers present throughout the facility with no labeling present. Ensure that all working food containers are labeled.

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.112 Remove dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed large amount of dead pests present in the rear of the facility. Pest were in traps as well as in back maintenance closet. Ensure that all dead insects are removed and pest control measures are increased.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed boxes of food present on the floor of the WIF. Ensure that all food is kept off of the floor in the facility. -CDI- Food boxes placed on shelving.

2-402.11 Use head coverings, beard guards and clothing to restrain body hair from contacting exposed food, equipment, and utensils. Observed food employee cooking in the kitchen with no head/hair covering. Ensure that all food employees used hair restraints.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed outside storage in the back of the facility. With boxes of single service articles. Single service articles were stored directly on the floor. Ensure that all single service articles are kept off of the floor.

violations.description: Warewashing facilities: installed, maintained & used; test strips
violations.comments:
4-303.11 (A) (B) Cleaning agents and chemical sanitizers that are used to clean and sanitize equipment and utensils shall be provided and available for use during all hours of operation. -Pf- Observed 3-comp sink with a buildup of dishes and no sanitizer hooked up to the sanitizer dispensing hose of the 3-comp sink. Ensure that chemical sanitizers are present at 3-comp sink at all hours of operation in the ware washing areas of the facility. 
-10 DAY VR- 
Ensure 3-comp sanitizer dispenser is restocked with sanitizer and maintained as stocked. 
Facility has bleach solution and test strips to manually use for the time being.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues.
Observed heavy buildup of dust and debris present on the external surfaces of equipment throughout the facility. Cooking equipment, shelving, and condenser fans in WIC all in need of cleaning. 
Ensure that non-food contact surfaces throughout the facility are cleaned and maintained as clean.

violations.description: Garbage & refuse properly disposed; facilities maintained
violations.comments: 5-501.113(B) Keep dumpster, outside waste containers covered with tight-fitting lids or doors. Observed dumpster doors open in the dumpster area. Ensure that dumpster doors are closed when not in use.

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed floors in several parts of the facility such as by the dish machine exposed concrete. Ensure that facilities floors are maintained as non-absorbent and smooth/easily cleanable.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-403.11 (A)Areas designated for employees to eat and drink, shall be located so that food, equipment, linens, single service/use items are protected from contamination. Observed employee food present in the lowboy unit stored directly above patron food. Ensure that employee food is stored in designated areas below patron food. -CDI- PIC moved food so that it was no longer above patron food.

rows.general_comments: For a reinspection call the main office at 980-314-1620

violations.comments: 4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf. Observed deli slicer and panini press in Betty’s market with visible food debris present. EHS advised PIC to clean deli slicer then cover it to maintain cleanliness when not use. Obsevred dirty dishes being stored in hot holding unit in Betty’s market. Observed dishes stored as clean in dish room with visible food debris present. EHS observed dishes being stored in container with screws, the nozzle to a hose (visibly dirty) and food debris was present at the bottom of the containers. CDI- Items were taken to dish to be w/r/s. **REPEAT** -3-

3-501.16(A)(1) Maintain TCS foods in hot holding at 135F or above. -P Observed pozole hot holding in soup well at 124F. Observed chicken patty holding at 125 in hot holding equipment at mecklenburger station. CDI- Soup was reheated to 165F and placed back on the line for service and chicken patty was voluntarily discarded. -1.5-
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P Observed cut tomatoes, sliced provolone, slcied pepper jack and sliced American cheese (x2) holding above 41F on service line (Mecklenburger). CDI- PIC placed items in shallow pan and placed in walk in cooler to cool down to 41F or below. -0- 

For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed sliced tomatoes and sliced turkey with a prep date of 1/20 (Betty’s Market) which is past the 7-day allotment for TCS items. CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded the items. **REPEAT** -1.5-

violations.description: Time as a Public Health Control; procedures & records
violations.comments: 3-501.19(B)(3) Follow written procedures for foods held using Time as a Public Health Control (TPHC) for 4 hours. Food shall be marked to indicate the time that is 4 hours past the point in time when the food is removed from temperature control. -Pf- Observed no time stamp for items being held on time at Betty’s market. -CDI- PIC properly labeled items. EHS advised that event though the service period is 3 hours, items held on TPHC must still be labeled **REPEAT** -3-

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed multiple squeeze bottles and seasoning containers without labels throughout facility. **REPEAT** -2-

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments: 6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed gnats throughout the facility.. **REPEAT**

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments: 4-903.11(B)(1) Clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows for air drying. Observed a few plastic containers in the dish area that are clean and stacked wet. **REPEAT** -0.5-

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed exterior of equipment in need of general cleaning in mecklenburger station. Observed food debris in the bottom of equipment in Betty’s market. EHS advised that if equipment is no longer in use to w/r/s equipment and protect it or remove from the facility. **REPEAT** -1-

6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed flooring lifting in dry storage area. Repair. Observed missing ceiling tiles throughout dry storage areas. replace tiles. -0-

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments: 6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee belongings stored with and on top of clean dishes in dish area **REPEAT** -1-

rows.general_comments:
PIC stated that oysters are no longer offered. 

No consumer advisory provided on breakfast menu but is supplied on regular menu which is always given in conjunction with breakfast menu. 

RCP will be implemented for date marking. 

For re-inspection, please call 980-314-1620.

violations.description: PIC Present, demonstrates knowledge, & performs duties
violations.comments:
2-103.11(A-P) PIC shall ensure rules in the code for food safety and handling are met. -Pf- Observed multiple risk factor violations during inspection. **10 DAY VERIFICATION REQUIRED BY 2/1/2026** -1-
A Food Safety Checklist can be found at: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety along with more food safety information related to PIC Duties, Training, and managing food safety

violations.description: Management, food & conditional employee; knowledge, responsibilities & reporting
violations.comments:
2-103.11(O) Ensure food employees are informed of their responsibility to report required symptoms, illnesses and exposure. -Pf- Observed no signed attestation of EHP. -CDI- EHS provided copy and it was signed by all employees at time of visit. -0-
For more information on employee health policy, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety

violations.description: Procedures for responding to vomiting & diarrheal events
violations.comments:
2-501.11 A food establishment shall have written procedures for employees to follow when responding to vomiting or diarrheal events. -Pf- Observed no V&D cleanup procedures. -CDI- EHS provided copy. -0-
For more information please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
6-301.14 Post a handwash sign at each handsink. Observed no handwashing sign at handsink at BOH near dishpit. -CDI- EHS provided signage. -0-

violations.description: Food in good condition, safe & unadulterated
violations.comments: 3-101.11 Food shall be safe for consumption, unadulterated and honestly presented. -P- Observed white and black microbial growth on jalapenos, ketchup and boiled eggs in WIC. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items. -1-

3-302.11(A)(1) Separate raw animal foods from ready-to-eat foods and washed/unwashed fruits and vegetables. -P- Observed bag of raw chorizo stored in container on top of bag of cooked chicken. Observed raw chorizo stored in container with ranch and queso in prep unit. -CDI- PIC corrected storage order. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation
3-302.11 (A)(4) Except when the food is being cooled, store food in packages, covered containers, or wrappings. Observed multiple items in RIFs, RIC, and WIC with no protective covering. **REPEAT** -3-
3-302.11(A)(8) Separate unwashed produce from ready-to-eat foods. Observed unwashed produce stored over washed RTE tomatoes in WIC. 
For more information on food storage order please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation

4-601.11(A) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be clean to sight and touch. -Pf.- Observed food debris on dishes stored as clean. Per PIC, some items not in use. -CDI- PIC moved items to dishpit to be W/R/S. Items not in use must be stored clean or removed from facility. -0-
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.11 (C ) Equipment food contact surfaces and utensils shall be cleaned, and sanitized per 4-702.11/4-703.11, throughout the day at least every 4 hours, unless otherwise indicated in the Food Code. -P- Observed in-use utensils that per PIC, had been in-use since facility opened at 9am (time of observation was at 2p). -CDI- EHS educated PIC on cleaning frequency and items were placed at dishpit to be W/R/S. 
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: Sanitizing Solutions - https://youtu.be/ykP2mr9CtRg , How to Test Sanitizer https://youtu.be/y0SjmnabFaY , and Cleaning and Sanitizing Food Contact Surfaces https://youtu.be/av1Rbh-lh08 .
4-602.12 (B) The cavities and door seals of microwave ovens shall be cleaned at least every 24 hours by using the manufacturer's recommended cleaning procedure. Observed microwave in need of cleaning due to food splash. Per PIC, microwave is cleaned every 2 days. Ensure microwave is being cleaned daily.

3-501.16(A)(2) Maintain TCS foods in cold holding at 41F or less. -P- Observed crema in prep unit #1 above 41F. Observed items in prep unit #2 on right side of unit above 41F. Observed items in WIC above 41F. Ambient air of WIC at 40-41F. However, most foods are not maintaining temperatures at 41F or below. Refer to temperature chart. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items above 41F. **VERIFICATION REQUIRED BY 1/23/26**
For more information, please visit: www.mecknc.gov/foodsafety or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q0iLZb7v0UM and Safe Food Temperatures https://youtu.be/tXP09IK-1TQ .

3-501.17 (A) or (B) Date mark/label all TCS foods that are ready-to-eat once opened or prepared and are to be held for more than 24 hours. -Pf. Establish a date marking system consistent with 3-501.17 (A) or (B). Additional date marking system options are found in 3-501.17 (D)./3-501.18 Discard the food requiring date labels once time/temperature window has expired, if it has not been labeled, or if the label is incorrect. -P The PIC should train and assign staff to monitor date marking to prevent the use of food products that are no longer in compliance with 3-501.17 (A), (B), or (C). Observed items missing date marks: cooked frozen beef, chicken, hot pickled onions, shredded mozzarella cheese, queso fresco, sauce containing mayo and whole milk, sliced tomatoes, cut cabbage, salad mix, red sauce, salsa verde, chip salsa, flautas, housemade drink containing milk, and carnitas. Observed items past date mark: cut cabbage 12/29 (discard 1/4), and queso 12/25 & 12/28 (discard 12/31 & 1/3). Observed items improperly date marked: corn, carrots and cut cabbage 1/18-1/26 (discard should be 1/24), and beans and seafood soup 1/13-1/23 (discard should be 1/19). Per PIC, items with no date mark were prepared 1/19. -CDI- PIC voluntarily discarded items past date mark and properly date marked other items. **REPEAT** -3- **RCP BEING IMPLEMENTED FOR DATE MARKING.**
For more information, please visit: https://health.mecknc.gov/envhealth/food-and-facilities-sanitation or view our training videos at the following weblinks: What are TCS Foods https://youtu.be/bRvqAETDjQU and Date Marking Food Safely https://youtu.be/BOPsSNUajYs .

3-302.12 Label all working containers of food (oils, spices, salts, flour, sugar) except food that is easy to identify such as dry pasta. Observed multiple squeeze bottles with no labels. **REPEAT** -2-

violations.description: Insects & rodents not present; no unauthorized animals
violations.comments:
6-202.15 Protect outer openings of establishment from insect or rodent entry. Observed back door not fully closed. Ensure door is closed to protect establishment from insect or rodent entry. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.111 Keep the premises free of insects, rodents, and other pests. Observed dead pest in cold pan in prep unit. Observed flying pest in RIC being used for storage.

violations.description: Contamination prevented during food preparation, storage & display
violations.comments: 3-305.11 Store food in a clean, dry location, not exposed to contamination. Keep at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed cokes and waters stored on floor in dry storage area. -0-

3-304.12 (F) Containers of water used for storing in-use utensils must be maintained at a temperature of at least 135 F and cleaned at least every 24 hours or when container has accumulated soil or residue. Observed in-use utensils being held in water at 80F. -0-

violations.description: Utensils, equipment & linens: properly stored, dried & handled
violations.comments:
4-903.11 (B) After cleaning and sanitizing, clean equipment and utensils shall be stored in a self-draining position that allows air drying and covered or inverted. Observed wet stacking with dishes stored as clean. -0-
4-903.11 (A) Store cleaned equipment, utensils, linens in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed clean tongs stored on hook where spray nozzle to rinse dirty dishes is stored.

violations.description: Single-use & single-service articles: properly stored & used
violations.comments: 4-903.11(A)(C) Store single-use and single-service articles in a clean, dry location; where they are not exposed to splash, dust, or other contamination; and at least 6 inches above the floor. Observed to go plates stored on floor in dry storage area. -0-

violations.description: Equipment, food & non-food contact surfaces approved, cleanable, properly designed, constructed & used
violations.comments:
4-501.12 Resurface or replace cutting surfaces that can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized. Observed cutting boards throughout no longer smooth and easily cleanable. Resurface or replace. 
4-101.11 (A) - (E ) Food contact surfaces shall be (A) Safe P, (B) Durable, Corrosion-Resistant, (C ) sufficient in weight and thickness to withstand repeated washing (D) Smooth and easily cleanable, and (E) Resistant to damage. Observed grocery bags being used on raw fish and other items. 
4-101.19/4-202.16 Non FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent 
cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Observed rust buildup on bottom shelves of 2 prep tables. Observed rusted shelving throughout in need of replacement. Observed shelving used to store clean dishes that had been spray painted. **REPEAT** -1-
4-502.11(A) Maintain utensils in good repair. Observed utensils with damaged/warped handles. 
4-501.11 Maintain equipment in good repair. Observed broken door handle gasket at RIF near WIC. Observed door on prep unit #1 not properly closing. 
4-205.10 Ensure equipment other than toasters, mixers, microwaves, water heaters and hoods is ANSI certified or equivalent. Observed nutri bullet that read “for household use only”.

4-601.11(C ) Keep non food contact surfaces of equipment clean to avoid build up of dust, food residue or debris.4-602.13 Non food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned frequently to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Observed tops and sides of equipment in need of cleaning throughout, especially cooking equipment and shelving in need of cleaning throughout. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. -0-

violations.description: Hot & cold water available; adequate pressure
violations.comments: 5-103.12 Provide water under pressure to all fixtures. Observed no water coming out at handsink by cook line. -CDI- PIC repaired. -0-

6-101.11/6-201.11 Floors, walls and ceilings shall be designed, constructed, and installed so they are smooth and easily cleanable. Floors shall be non-absorbent in areas subject to moisture. Observed cracked and lifting floor tiles near the back door. Also observed multiple missing baseboards throughout the facility (Kitchen, back storage room, and WIC). Observed ceiling tile above RIC in need of replacement due to hole.
6-501.114 (A) The premises shall be free of items that are unnecessary to the operation or maintenance of the establishment such as equipment that is nonfunctional or no longer used. Observed broken RI unit and lowboy being used for storage. Observed plates and some cold pans that per PIC were no longer in use. Remove any items no longer in use. **REPEAT** -1-
6-501.12 Floors, walls, ceilings including the attachments such as soap, towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vent covers shall be clean. Caution should be used to minimize food exposure. Observed debris buildup on tops of paper towel dispensers. Observed food debris and water on floors. Increased and more thorough cleaning frequency recommended. 
6-501.11 Floors, walls, and ceilings including the attachments such as soap and towel dispensers; light fixtures; and heat/ac vents shall be maintained in good repair. Observed most lights with no light shields and one light shield hanging down, not fully intact. Replace.

violations.description: Meets ventilation & lighting requirements; designated areas used
violations.comments:
6-305.11/6-501.110 Designate and use an area for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and possessions. Observed employee items stored over retail items in RIC. Observed employee coffee machine stored on top of table next to small cold holding unit where sauces are held. Observed bible stored on shelving above prep unit with other retail items. **REPEAT** -0.5-
6-303.11 Provide required lighting intensity. Observed little to no lighting at prep units and at cook/grill line.
